greeting: GET ME OUT OF HERE! NOW!

responses: I am a great and famous actor! Not a princess, at all. I was only PRETENDING to be a princess. But try explaining that to those stupid pirates.
Stage acting was a waste of my immense talent. Not only am I a born leader, my talent is more profitable when it is used for conning people.
Stage acting was a waste of my immense talent. Not only am I a born leader, my talent is more profitable when it is used for conning people.
He was always a fool with a heart too soft to become a feared pirate.
Me playing a princess was just part of a cunning plan we had.
If you find some way to release me I might even let you live as reward! So you'd better do your best or I'll kill you!
How dare you? I left to rot in this dirty cell and you have nothing better to do than chit chat?
YOU .. YOU .. You are as good as dead! I will get you! Do you hear me? I will have your head! On a platter!
In a just world, I would be captain of a grand ship, ...
Those pirates out there would now be my minions, and we would brave the seas and become the terror of the coastal towns! ...
If only our plan had worked!
Captain Kid sold his ship to buy pointless things like those insanely expensive locks for the cell doors. ...
He said the canoes would do for a while. ...
I got the impression he was not overly sad to part with the ship because he was known to suffer a lot from seasickness.
I'd have been a much better captain then Kid was. I played several captains on stage and I was good! ...
Where Kid longed for the appreciation of his men, I would rule by fear and with an iron fist!
It was all captain Kid's idea. You see, he hated his name and planned to become known by the name captain Kidnap. ...
All he needed was someone famous to kidnap. ...
Given his men's dismal lack of talent and intelligence that would have been quite a feat. ...
We knew each other from a few scams we did together in the past, so he contacted me. ...
I was to impersonate the famous Princess Lumelia. You know, the one everyone was looking for. ...
That would show his men and the other pirates what a great kidnapper he was. ...
He promised me that I would become his second in command and lead a wonderful life of plundering, robbing and pillaging. ...
So I agreed to impersonate the Princess for a while and it worked fine at first. ...
He returned with me dressed as the Princess from a raid on his own and was instantaneously the hero of the day for his men. ...
Things went bad when they decided to have a victory party. ...
As far as I could make out from the mumblings of the pirates, Kid lost the key to my cell while relieving himself in the underground river. ...
The fool decided to dive after it .. never to be seen again. ...
When I found out about Kid's demise I tried to convince the pirates it was a hoax, but they just won't believe me!
Ah kidnapping is so much fun. That is, if you're not on the receiving end. ...
It's easy money and you have a chance to frighten and torture someone who can't fight back!
The more stupid the people are, the easier it is to con them. ...
And the poorer they are the less means they have to get revenge. Har Har! ...
So I make sure I ruin those I scam. Then they have other things to worry about than getting revenge on me.
The key was lost in the underground river and has probably washed into the seven seas by now! ...
If that stupid Kid hadn't been so obsessed with kidnapping he'd not have sold his ship to buy the most expensive and complicated locks for his cells!
As long as we stick to undefended coastal towns we can make an easy fortune. Har Har! ...
As soon as I get out of here I'll finally become a pirate captain on my own. I don't need Captain Kid!

greeting: Hi! What is it, what d'ye {want}?

farewell: Good bye and don't forget me!
walkAway: Good bye and don't forget me!

farewell: Good bye and don't forget me!
walkAway: Good bye and don't forget me!

farewell: Good bye and don't forget me!
walkAway: Good bye and don't forget me!

farewell: Good bye and don't forget me!
walkAway: Good bye and don't forget me!

responses: Chchch. Chh! <you're not sure, but it seems that squirrel wants to trade your valuable acorns for useless stones that it found and considered uneatable>
Chh? Chhh?? <though you don't understand squirrelish, that one seems really excited>

greeting: Ah, I feel a mortal walks these ancient halls again. Pardon me, I barely notice you. I am so lost in my thoughts.

responses: You will now be travelled out of here. Are you sure that you want to face that teleport?
Then stay here in these ghostly halls.
I can offer you a {teleport}.
Dont mind me.

greeting: I feel you. I hear your thoughts. You are ... alive.

responses: Once I was a member of the order of the nightmare knights. Now I am but a shadow who walks these cold halls.
The north has a puzzle to complete.

greeting: Interesting. A human who can speak the language of wolves.
Huh? What? I can see! Wow! A non-mino. Did they {capture} you as well?
farewell: Good bye! Don't forget about the secrets of mathemagics.
walkAway: Wait! Don't leave! I want to tell you about my surreal numbers.

greeting: Hi? What do you mean 'hi' - can't you see I am a... I have to lower my voice. Did it occur to you, that this 'chalice' does not want to be disturbed?! Leave me alone!

responses: 'Alice the chalice'... Hmpf, of course I won't TELL you my name, I am UNDERCOVER! Now be quiet or they will hear you.
Well, I am drinking most of the time. Er, no I mean - trying to hold my fluids... ah, wait that came out wrong: I am doing research. Let's just keep it at that.
I am smith.
Working steel is my profession.
Manny kinds of. Like {Mesh Kaha Rogh'}, {Za'Kalortith}, {Uth'Byth}, {Uth'Morc}, {Uth'Amon}, {Uth'Maer}, {Uth'Doon}, and {Zatragil}.
Most ancients use dream silver for different stuff. Now ancients most gone. Most not know about.
It's high steel called. Only lil' lil' ones know how make.
It's evil. Demon iron is. No good cyclops goes where you can find and need evil flame to melt.
Steel that is singing when forged. No one knows where find today.
Not good to make stuff off. Bad steel it is. But eating magic, so useful is.
Brightsteel is. Much art made with it. Sorcerers too lazy and afraid to enchant much.
Heartiron from heart of big old mountain, found very deep. Lil' lil ones fiercely defend. Not wanting to have it used for stuff but holy stuff.
Me parents live here before town was. Me not care about lil' ones.
Lil' lil' ones are so fun. We often chat.
One day I'll go and look.
Is one of elven family or such thing. Me not understand lil' ones and their business.
Is one of elven family or such thing. Me not understand lil' ones and their business.
I called Bencthyclthrtrprr by me people. Lil' ones me call Big Ben.
You shut up. Me not want to hear.
Do lil' one want to trade a fire sword?
Do lil' one want to trade a dragon shield?
Do lil' one want to trade a sword of valor?
Do lil' one want to trade a warlord sword?
They were friend with me parents. Long before elves here, they often made visit. No longer come here.
Me not fight them, they not fight me.
Me wish I could make weapon like it.
Me people not live here much. Most are far away.

greeting: I greet you, human. This is a time of distress, more than ever are we in need of guardians to protect us and our world.

responses: My name is now known only to the wind and it shall remain like this until I will return to my kin.
I was a guardian of this glade. I am the last one... everyone had to leave.
This glade's time is growing short if nothing will be done soon.
Take care, guardian.
Cruel beings. Large and monstrous, with a single eye, staring at their prey.

greeting: What is this? An optically challenged entity called |PLAYERNAME|. How fascinating!
farewell: Wait right there. I will eat you after writing down what I found out.
walkAway: Wait right there. I will eat you after writing down what I found out.

responses: Yes, yes, I will kill you soon enough, now let me continue my investigation on you.
They will mourn the day they abandoned us.
I am the great librarian.
It's a fine library, isn't it?
I am 486486 and NOT 'Blinky' as some people called me ... before they died.
It's 1, not 'Tibia', silly.
Uglyness incarnate. One eye! Imagine that! Horrible!
These fools and their superstitious life cult don't understand anything of importance.
Only inferior species need weapons.
I heard that elves moved in upstairs.
Numbers are essential. They are the secret behind the scenes. If you are a master of mathematics you are a master over life and death.
Our books are written in 469, of course you can't understand them.
Go and wash your eyes for using this obscene number!
The language of my kind. Superior to any other language and only to be spoken by entities with enough eyes to blink it.
Noisy pests.
Their mages are so close to the truth. Closer then they know and closer then it's good for them.
Good tools to work with ... After their death, that is.

You pitiful two-eyed creatures. In our eyes, you look weird. It is pathetic how much you depend on hands and legs. Since we bonelords have more eyes than any creature in the world, it is obvious that you can determine the value of a species by the number of its eyes.

Our race is very old. Over the time, we have been given many different names by other races. The term bonelord sticks to us for quite a while now. In our language the name of our race is not fix but a complex formula, and as such it always changes for the subjective viewer.

Our language is beyond comprehension by your lesser beings. It heavily relies on mathemagic. Your brain is not suited for the mathemagical processing necessary to understand our language.To decipher even our most basic texts, it would need a genius that can calculate numbers within seconds in his brain.

greeting: Be mourned pilgrim in flesh. I'm selling general goods. Just ask me for a {trade}.

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Hello, hello, |PLAYERNAME|! Please come in, look, and buy! I'm a specialist for all sorts of {tools}. Just ask me for a {trade} to see my offers! You can also ask me for general {hints} about the game. ...
You can also ask me about each {citizen} of the isle.

responses: Take a look in the trade window to your left.
Just ask me for a {trade} to see what I buy from you.
Just ask me for a {trade} to see my offers.
If you need general {equipment}, just ask me for a {trade}. I can also provide you with some general {hints} about the game.
As an adventurer, you should always have at least a {backpack}, a {rope}, a {shovel}, a {weapon}, an {armor} and a {shield}.
I'm fine. I'm so glad to have you here as my customer.
I'm a merchant. Just ask me for a {trade} to see my offers.
My name is Al Dee, but you can call me Al. Can I interest you in a {trade}?
It's about |TIME|. I'm so sorry, I have no watches to sell. Do you want to buy something else?
If you want to challenge monsters in the {dungeons}, you need some {weapons} and {armor} from the local {merchants}.
If you want to explore the dungeons such as the {sewers}, you have to {equip} yourself with the vital stuff I am selling. It's {vital} in the deepest sense of the word.
Oh, our sewer system is very primitive - it's so primitive that it's overrun by {rats}. But the stuff I sell is safe from them. Just ask me for a {trade} to see it!
The king encouraged salesmen to travel here, but only I dared to take the risk, and a risk it was!
Bugs plague this isle, but my wares are bug-free, totally bug-free.
One day I will return to the continent as a rich, a very rich man!
Thais is a crowded town.

Have you ever wondered what that 'main' is people are talking about? Well, once you've reached level 8, you should talk to the {oracle}. You can choose a {profession} afterwards and explore much more of Tibia.
Oh, I'm sorry, but I don't deal with weapons. That's {Obi's} or {Lee'Delle's} business. I could offer you a {pick} in exchange for a {small axe} if you should happen to own one.
Armor and shields can be bought at {Dixi's} or at {Lee'Delle's}. Dixi runs that shop near {Obi's}.
Armor and shields can be bought at {Dixi's} or at {Lee'Delle's}. Dixi runs that shop near {Obi's}.
I you want to find someone who may want to buy your cookies, you should meet Lily.
Blueberries grow on bushes. They're quite common in Tibia. Just pick them from a bush if you need a snack!
Sorry, I don't sell potions. You should visit {Lily} for that.
Hmm, the best address to look for food might be {Willie} or {Billy}. {Norma} also has some snacks for sale.
A bank is quite useful. You can deposit your money safely there. This way you don't have to carry it around with you all the time. You could also invest your money in my {wares}!
The big building in the centre of Rookgaard. They have a library, a training centre, a {bank} and the room of the {oracle}. {Seymour} is the teacher there.
The monk {Cipfried} takes care of our temple. He can heal you if you're badly injured or poisoned.
As a premium adventurer you have many advantages. You really should check them out!
If you tell me the name of a citizen, I'll tell you what I know about him or her.
To view the offers of a merchant, simply talk to him or her and ask for a {trade}. They will gladly show you their offers and also the things they buy from you.
You will learn everything you need to know about professions once you've reached the {Island of Destiny}.
The Island of Destiny can be reached via the {oracle} once you are level 8. This trip will help you choose your {profession}!
No thank you. I can already overstock the market with torches.
I sell fishing rods and worms if you want to fish. Simply ask me for a {trade}.
Yes, I am selling that. Simply ask me for a {trade} to view all my offers.
He is just an old monk. However, he can heal you if you are badly injured or poisoned.
Poor old woman, her son {Tom} never visits her.
He dedicated his life to welcome newcomers to this island.
No idea who that is.
Yep, that's me. Smart of you to notice that!
He's the local {bank} clerk.
He mostly stays by himself. He's a hermit outside of town - good luck finding him.
She's {Obi's} granddaughter and deals with {armors} and {shields}. Her shop is south west of town, close to the {temple}.
He sells {weapons}. His shop is south west of town, close to the {temple}.
If you are a {premium} adventurer, you should check out {Lee'Delle's} shop. She lives in the western part of town, just across the bridge.
He's the local tanner. You could try selling fresh corpses or leather to him.
She's currently recovering from her travels in the {academy}. It's always nice to chat with her!
You can find the oracle on the top floor of the {academy}, just above {Seymour}. Go there when you are level 8.
Seymour is a teacher running the {academy}. He has many important {information} about Tibia.
She sells health {potions} and antidote potions. Also, she buys {blueberries} and {cookies} in case you find any.
This is a local farmer. If you need fresh {food} to regain your health, it's a good place to go. However, many monsters also carry food such as meat or cheese. Or you could simply pick {blueberries}.
This is a local farmer. If you need fresh {food} to regain your health, it's a good place to go. He's only trading with {premium} adventurers though.
She used to sell equipment, but I think she has opened a small bar now. Talks about changing her name to 'Mary' and such, strange girl.
Some call him a hero. He protects the town from {monsters}.
Picks are hard to come by. I trade them only in exchange for high quality small axes. Would you like to make that deal?
Sorry, I am looking for a SMALL axe.
Well, then don't.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Greetings, pilgrim. Welcome to the halls of hope. We are the keepers of this {temple} and welcome everyone willing to contribute.

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.
I am Albinius, a worshipper of the {Astral Shapers}.
Precisely time.
I find ways to unveil the secrets of the stars. Judging by this question, I doubt you follow my weekly publications concerning this research.
Of course, just browse through my wares.
At your service, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Hi there |PLAYERNAME|, and welcome to the {magic} store.

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.
I'm selling runes, life rings, wands, rods and crystal balls. I also buy powerful spellbooks. If you like to see my offers, ask me for a {trade}.

greeting: Welcome, young |PLAYERNAME|! If you are heavily wounded or poisoned, I can {heal} you for free.
walkAway: Remember: If you are heavily wounded or poisoned, I can heal you for free.

responses: This is a special blessing I can bestow upon you once you have obtained at least one of the other blessings and which functions a bit differently. ...
It only works when you're killed by other adventurers, which means that at least half of the damage leading to your death was caused by others, not by monsters or the environment. ...

The {twist of fate} will not reduce the death penalty like the other blessings, but instead prevent you from losing your other blessings as well as the amulet of loss, should you wear one. It costs the same as the other blessings. ...
Would you like to receive that protection for a sacrifice of |PVPBLESSCOST| gold, child?
Fine. You are free to decline my offer.
Ah, you want to replace your adventurer's stone for 30 gold?
Sorry, you don't have enough money.
No problem.
You aren't looking that bad. Sorry, I can't help you. But if you are looking for additional protection you should go on the {pilgrimage} of ashes or get the protection of the {twist of fate} here.

Whenever you receive a lethal wound, your vital force is damaged and there is a chance that you lose some of your equipment. With every single of the five {blessings} you have, this damage and chance of loss will be reduced.

There are five blessings available in five sacred places: the {spiritual} shielding, the spark of the {phoenix}, the {embrace} of Tibia, the fire of the {suns} and the wisdom of {solitude}. Additionally, you can receive the {twist of fate} here.
You can ask for the blessing of spiritual shielding in the whiteflower temple south of Thais.
The druids north of Carlin will provide you with the embrace of Tibia.
You can ask for the blessing of the two suns in the suntower near Ab'Dendriel.
The spark of the phoenix is given by the dwarven priests of earth and fire in Kazordoon.
Talk to the hermit Eremo on the isle of Cormaya about this blessing.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Nature's blessing, traveller! May you not be affected by any sinister force.
farewell: May your path always be even.
walkAway: May your path always be even.

responses: Outside of our secret {realm} my siblings and I can't keep our true shape. If we want to travel other parts of the world, we must take over the bodies of animals. But we are causing them no harm and we just take control if necessary.

We call it Feyrist and it is a secret, hidden place. Just few mortals get permission to enter it. A long time ago, we learned how to hide our realm from the outside world. Only if you gain our trust I will tell you how to reach it.

We call ourselves the fae. Some name us nature spirits or peri but we prefer the former term. Most of us are rather reclusive and live peaceful lives in our secret realm. We only leave it in order to {protect} our home. ...

We call ourselves the fae. Some name us nature spirits or peri but we prefer the former term. Most of us are rather reclusive and live peaceful lives in our secret realm. We only leave it in order to {protect} our home. ...
I can sense a kind of dark energy lately. It is pervading this world, more and more every day. Yet I don't know where it arises from nor what we could do to dispel it.
It is rather subversive, so most creatures won't sense it ... yet. But its corrosive power has already begun to affect my kind and our hidden realm in unpleasant ways.
Some call us nature spirits or peri but we prefer the term fae. Most of us are rather reclusive and live peaceful lives in our secret realm. We only leave it in order to protect our home. ...

We tend to be secretive about our true nature, but I guess there was once an elven sage who visited our realm and put his experiences down on paper. There might be a book about the fae in the library of Ab'Dendriel.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Kneel before the all-devouring power of blooded decay.

responses: Find the Golden Anchor and click there, after talk with Navigator.

greeting: Welcome, young |PLAYERNAME|! If you are heavily wounded or poisoned, I can {heal} you for free.
walkAway: Remember: If you are heavily wounded or poisoned, I can heal you for free.

responses: Your mission here on Tibia is to be polite and friendly. The gods will reward you, I promise!
This is a special blessing I can bestow upon you once you have obtained at least one of the other blessings and which functions a bit differently. ...
It only works when you're killed by other adventurers, which means that at least half of the damage leading to your death was caused by others, not by monsters or the environment. ...

The {twist of fate} will not reduce the death penalty like the other blessings, but instead prevent you from losing your other blessings as well as the amulet of loss, should you wear one. It costs the same as the other blessings. ...
Would you like to receive that protection for a sacrifice of |PVPBLESSCOST| gold, child?
Fine. You are free to decline my offer.
Ah, you want to replace your adventurer's stone for 30 gold?
Sorry, you don't have enough money.
No problem.
A blessed stake? That's a strange request. Maybe Quentin knows more, he is one of the oldest monks after all.
You aren't looking that bad. Sorry, I can't help you. But if you are looking for additional protection you should go on the {pilgrimage} of ashes or get the protection of the {twist of fate} here.

Whenever you receive a lethal wound, your vital force is damaged and there is a chance that you lose some of your equipment. With every single of the five {blessings} you have, this damage and chance of loss will be reduced.

There are five blessings available in five sacred places: the {spiritual} shielding, the spark of the {phoenix}, the {embrace} of Tibia, the fire of the {suns} and the wisdom of {solitude}. Additionally, you can receive the {twist of fate} here.
You can ask for the blessing of spiritual shielding in the whiteflower temple south of Thais.
The druids north of Carlin will provide you with the embrace of Tibia.
You can ask for the blessing of the two suns in the suntower near Ab'Dendriel.
The spark of the phoenix is given by the dwarven priests of earth and fire in Kazordoon.
Talk to the hermit Eremo on the isle of Cormaya about this blessing.

greeting: Oh hello, nice to see you |PLAYERNAME|. Are you here to hear some stories of my {adventures} or do you need {help}?

responses: I'm recovering from a {sea} passage.
I'm an {explorer} who seeks {adventures}.
I've been almost everywhere in {Tibia}.
I fought fierce {monsters}, climbed the highest mountains, explored the deepest {dungeons} and crossed the {sea} on a {raft}.
My voyage on the sea was exhausting. The weather was bad, the waves high and my raft quite simple. There's a certain excitement to it, though.
Sorry, I lost my watch in a storm.
Well, I can give you general {hints} or tell you about my {adventures} and many other topics. Oh, and if you are bored, I might have a small {quest} for you.
Well, I can give you general {hints} or tell you about my {adventures} and many other topics. Oh, and if you are bored, I might have a small {quest} for you.
I had no time to explore the dungeons of this isle yet, but I've seen two big caves in the East, and there is a ruined tower to the north-west. Oh, and there're the {sewers}.
I like sewers. I made my very first battle experience in the sewers below {Thais}. The small sewer system of {Rookgaard} has some nasty rats to fight.
Oh, I fought {orcs}, {cyclopses}, {minotaurs}, even {dragons}, and many other creatures.
I don't like the way they look at you. Their eye seems to pierce right through you. Creepy!
Their breath is so hot! I had to cut my hair after my last encounter with a dragon because the ends were all burnt. That's what you gotta deal with as a female adventurer!
I left my raft at the south-eastern shore. I forgot my private {notebook} on it. If you could return it to me, I would be very grateful.
I left my raft at the south-eastern shore. I forgot my private {notebook} on it. If you could return it to me, I would be very grateful.
I left my raft at the south-eastern shore. I forgot my private {notebook} on it. If you could return it to me, I would be very grateful.
I think this poor guy was a bad choice as head of the {academy}.
A fine institution, but it needs definitely more funds from the {king}.
King Tibianus is the ruler of {Thais}. The island of {Rookgaard} belongs to his kingdom.
A fine city, but the {king} has some problems enforcing the law.
The best weapons on this isle are just toothpicks compared with the weapons warriors wield on the {mainland}.
Spells are only taught in the guildhalls of the mainland.
I'm trying to explore each spot of Tibia, and one day I'll have seen it all.
If you travel to Thais, you really should visit the marvelous castle there.
You'll be surprised once you leave this island. The world outside there is gigantic.
The most important tools you need are a {rope}, a {shovel} and maybe a {torch}.
One day I fell into a hole without having a rope. I was yelling for help for three whole days! Eventually a fisherman passed by and pulled me out with his own rope, lucky me.
Sometimes I have the strong urge to use it to knock someone out.
You know, on mainland you will be able to cast magic spells which provide you with light. You won't really need torches anymore.
I have almost no money with me anyway, so no need to deposit any at the bank.
You will find your destiny. I'm sure it's something big and important.
A fine institution, but it needs definitely more funds from the {king}.
Oh, I'm sorry, but I'm not interested in buying or selling anything.
I wouldn't want to miss being a premium adventurer. Everything is so much easier!
I don't have much to say about him. I think he sells {tools}.
Never seen him around.
She seriously asked me if she could have the remains of my {raft} as fire wood! Can you imagine that??
He promised to repair my {raft}.
Did you know my name is also the name of a gem?
To me he seems a bit rude, but maybe that's just my impression.
I heard her offers are extraordinarily good.
The oracle is said to show you your {destiny} once you are level 8.
She has changed a lot since I last saw her.
I think this poor guy was a bad choice as head of the {academy}.
Hm, I think I haven't met her yet.
He brought me some of his famous rat stew. I really didn't want to insult him, but I simply can't eat something like that. So I told him I'm a vegetarian and I only eat fish. <gulps>
He's funny in his own way.
No, I didn't go to the {bank} yet.
A gentle person. You should visit him if you have questions or need healing.
Hyacinth is a great healer. He lives somewhere hidden on this isle.
He's a funny little man.
I don't really know her, but she seems to be a nice girl.
An extraordinary warrior. He's the first and last line of defense of {Rookgaard}.
Not the nicest guys you can encounter. I had some clashes with them and finally ended up being their {prisoner} for a few months.
I speak some Orcish words, not many though, just a few basics like '{yes}' and '{no}'.
It's 'mok' in Orcish. I tell you more if you bring me some {food}.
In Orcish that's 'burp'. I tell you more if you bring me some {food}.
My favorite dish is {salmon}. Oh please, bring me some.
Yeah! If you give me some salmon, I'll tell you another Orcish word. Okay?
You don't have any salmon!
Ok, then I won't tell you another Orcish word.
Do you bring me my notebook?
Mhm, whatever you have there, it is not my notebook.
Too bad.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Ahoi, young man |PLAYERNAME| and welcome to the Nordic Tibia Ferries. If you need a {passage}, let me know.

responses: Where do you want to go today? We serve the routes to Senja, {Folda} and {Vega} and back to {Tibia}.
We are ferrymen. We transport goods and passengers to the Ice Islands.
We are ferrymen. We transport goods and passengers to the Ice Islands.
Of course, just browse through my wares.
Of course, just browse through my wares.
I am the mistress of the hunt. At this place you may buy the food our hunts provide.
Hunting is an art, practiced too often by diletantes. Every fool with a bow or a spear considers himself a hunter.
Bow, arrow, and spear are the hunters' best friends. In the northeast of the town one of us may sell such tools.
Hunters live a life of freedom and closeness to nature, unlike a simple farmer or bugherder.
Nature is not a friend but an unforgiving teacher, and the lessons we have to learn are endless.
Watch the sky, it will tell you.
I guess it's a human god for the human sight of nature. I have not much knowledge of this entity.
If they ever existed they are gone now.
The Kuridai are too agressive not only to people but also to the enviroment. They lack any understanding of the balance that we know as nature.
The balance of nature, of course. It's everywhere, so don't ask but observe and learn.
We try to live in harmony with the forces of nature, may they be living or unliving.
The humans are a loud and ugly race. They lack any grace and are more kin to the orcs then to us.
Life and death are significant parts of the balance.
Life and death are significant parts of the balance.
I despise their presence in our town, but it may be a necessary evil.
That is the race to which I belong.
The magic they wield is all that matters to them.

I'm the overseer of the pits and a paladin trainer, but I also know some knight spells.
Those wannabe fighters are weak and most of them are unable to comprehend a higher concept like the Mooh'Tah.
I teach worthy warriors the way of the knight.
I'm known as Asrak the Ironhoof.
I pledge no allegiance to any king, be it human or minotaurean.
Your human army might be big but without skills. They are nothing than sheep to be slaughtered.
The human generals are like their warriors. They lack the focus to be true warriors.
The dungeons of your desires and fears are not only the solely ones you have to fear but also the only ones you have to conquer.
They implanted the rage in us that almost cost our existence. They used us as pawns in wars that were not ours.
Inferior creatures of rage, driven by their primitive urges. They are only useful to test one's skills.
To rely on magic is like cheating fate. All cheaters will get their just punishment one day, and so will he.
If it's truly a weapon to slay gods, it might be worth searching for it.
The city pays me well and those undisciplined gladiators need my skills and guidance badly.
The city is only a shadow of what we could have accomplished without that curse of rage that the gods bestowed upon us.
In the ancient wars we lost many things due to our rage. The only good thing is that we lost our trust in the gods, too.
The Mooh'Tah teaches control. It provides you with weapon, armor, and shield. It teaches you harmony and focus.
Rage is the legacy of Blog, the beast. It is our primary goal to overcome this rage. The Mooh'Tah is our only hope of salvation and perfection.
There is harmony in everything that is done correctly. If you feel the harmony of an action, you can sing its song.
Each harmonic action has it own song. If you can sing it, you are in harmony with that action. This is where the minotaurean battle songs come from.
Each Mooh'Tah master focuses his skills on the harmony of battle. He is one with the song that he's singing with his voice or at least his heart.
Make your will your weapon, and your enemies will perish.
Courage is the only armor that shields you against rage and fear, the greatest dangers you are facing.
Your confidence shall be your shield. Nothing can penetrate that defence.

This is a special blessing I can bestow upon you once you have obtained at least one of the other blessings and which functions a bit differently. ...
It only works when you're killed by other adventurers, which means that at least half of the damage leading to your death was caused by others, not by monsters or the environment. ...

The {twist of fate} will not reduce the death penalty like the other blessings, but instead prevent you from losing your other blessings as well as the amulet of loss, should you wear one. It costs the same as the other blessings. ...
Would you like to receive that protection for a sacrifice of |PVPBLESSCOST| gold, child?
Fine. You are free to decline my offer.
Ah, you want to replace your adventurer's stone for 30 gold?
Sorry, you don't have enough money.
No problem.
You aren't looking that bad. Sorry, I can't help you. But if you are looking for additional protection you should go on the {pilgrimage} of ashes or get the protection of the {twist of fate} here.

Whenever you receive a lethal wound, your vital force is damaged and there is a chance that you lose some of your equipment. With every single of the five {blessings} you have, this damage and chance of loss will be reduced.

There are five blessings available in five sacred places: the {spiritual} shielding, the spark of the {phoenix}, the {embrace} of Tibia, the fire of the {suns} and the wisdom of {solitude}. Additionally, you can receive the {twist of fate} here.
You can ask for the blessing of spiritual shielding in the whiteflower temple south of Thais.
The druids north of Carlin will provide you with the embrace of Tibia.
You can ask for the blessing of the two suns in the suntower near Ab'Dendriel.
The spark of the phoenix is given by the dwarven priests of earth and fire in Kazordoon.
Talk to the hermit Eremo on the isle of Cormaya about this blessing.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

I came here for peace and leisure, so leave me alone with 'time'.
Rather give your {money} to me than leaving it at the bank. Hehe.
Thank you, I'm fine.
If you need information, I can provide you with general {hints}.
I'm a cook, not a priest. If you need information, I can provide you with general {hints}.
I'm the best cook around. You can sell most types of {food} to me. Just ask me for a {trade} to see what I buy from you as well as my offers.
Which citizen?
If you bring me a fresh rat for my famous stew, ask me for a {trade}.
Nah, this is not a real shop. I have some offers, though. If you're interested, just ask me for a {trade}. There are other {merchants} on {Rookgaard} as well.
There is so much to explore! Better hurry to get to the continent! Once you're level 8, look for the {oracle}.
As an adventurer, you should always have at least a {backpack}, a {rope}, a {shovel}, a {weapon}, an {armor} and a {shield}.
There are lots and lots of dusty books. You can read them for some basic knowledge about the world. Then again, you could also ask around in the village.
The king and his tax collectors are far away. You'll meet them soon enough.
Ask {Obi} or {Lee'Delle}. They make a fortune here with all those wannabe heroes.
This island will prepare you for the main continent of {Tibia}. Learn to fight and grow stronger!
Don't be scared, you should be safe as long as you stay in town. Dangerous monsters are roaming the {dungeons}.
You'll find a lot of dungeons if you look around. One example are the {sewers} below Rookgaard.
The local sewers are invested by {rats}. Fresh rats give a good stew, you can sell them to me.
Just ask me for a {trade} to see what I buy from you. Food of almost any kind!
To view the offers of a merchant, simply talk to him or her and ask for a {trade}.
That's right, I'm the god of cooking!
I'd love to make pancakes, but I'm lacking a decent {pan}. If you get me one, I'll reward you.
Armor and shields can be bought at {Dixi's} or at {Lee'Delle's}. Dixi runs that shop near {Obi's}.
Get that from {Al Dee} or {Lee'Delle}.
Well, no gold, no deal. Earn gold by fighting {monsters} and picking up the things they carry. Sell it to {merchants} to make profit!
I can spell but I don't know any spells.
Just ask me for a {trade} to see my offers.
I like him, we usually have a drink or two once a week and share stories about {Willie}.
That girl is all about party now. I think she was fed up with {equipment} selling.
Now that's one crazy fellow. I usually hear him scream about some hole.
He's a fisherman and somehow his hut is always infested with cockroaches.
That old hag always finds someone to do her work. I have no clue how she is doing that.
Decent guy. He sells general equipment such as {ropes}, {shovels} and so on.
She's pretty indeed! I wonder if she likes bearded men.
Gotta love that name.
Don't listen to this old wannabe, I'm the best cook around.
He never leaves the temple. He spends his time caring for those who newly arrive here. You can ask him for a heal if you are badly injured or poisoned.
That's {Obi's} granddaughter. She helps him out by selling {armors} and {shields}. I think she'll be a beauty when she's grown up.
I never see that guy around.
Her shop is super-duper exclusive. If you're a premium adventurer, check it out in the western part of town. Lotsa nice offers there.
She lives in the very south of this town, selling her potions. Sweet - well, sweet-loving girl.
You can find the oracle on the top floor of the {academy}, just above {Seymour}. Go there when you are level 8.
He must have the most boring job there is.
I don't like his headmaster behaviour. Then again, he IS the headmaster of the {academy}.
Tom the tanner buys what's too nasty or too old to eat, animal-wise. Dead wolves and such.
He is one of the king's best men and here to protect us.
Have you found a pan for me?
Hey! You don't have one!
Then go and look for one!

farewell: Remember: A joke a day keeps the ghouls away!

responses: The good thing about them is that they can't be at two places at the same time.
I wonder if they love my water squirt flowers as much as all other plants.
They are the king's favourites, because they know how to 'bow'.
Did you notice that old knights have their scars just on their backs?
A fitting place for a {jester}. I guess there are worse {jobs} around.
Bozo: Nah, no jests about His Royal Highness.
I rarely leave the castle. It's really stressful to be as popular as me.
This castle is my home. A fitting place for a jester and all other fools. Feel welcome.
I'm a jester, not a doctor!
My name is Bozo. But it's more than a name, it's a lifestyle for me!
That's me: Bozo, the jester!
Ever since the first guild was created, there is a great demand of jesters and fools to join them.
Sell? Hmm, I know a little about magic and by chance I can sell you a truly unusual {weapon}.
I know some 'monstrous' jokes!
I know the newest jokes in tibia.
Thank you, I'm fine, the gods are with me.
Pheeew! That sounds disgusting! Are you a cook at Frodo's?
Don't feed the necromants.
Are we talking about Noodles?
Are we talking about Noodles?
Hey, the little one is almost as funny as me!
Better don't mess with sorcerers!
She's pretty but too serious for my taste.
Humourless old guy! Once, he turned me into a frog for painting his distasteful cave in pink.
A man of steel with a stomach of wax. Never offer him a beer!
He's my baby brother. If you tell him I sent you, he will grant you an extra spell or two.
He sells spell scrolls each day at midnight, but you have to address him that very second.
I better make no jokes about THIS matter.
Did you know that he sells a 'power axe of doom' now? Run and buy it, he only has got three in store.
He would make a fine jester, too.
I know a lot of monster jokes. Just tell me a monster's name, come on.
Why are the experienced heroes quicker than others? ... The demons love fast food!
Where do ghouls buy their robes? ... In a boooohtique!
Why do dragons breathe fire? ... They ate too many sorcerers in chilli sauce!
Why do orcs have green skin? ... They ate at Frodo's!
How many eyes does a cyclops have? ... One for each IQ point they have!
If you believe half the rumours he's spreading, you will get in a lot of trouble.
If you are a bad jester, you get a chance to visit them now and then.
What do all little minotaurs want to become when they are grown-ups? ... Cowboys, of course!
Why do trolls live underground? ... Because there are so many pks on the surface!
Why are bonelords so ugly? ... Because their mom and dad were bonelords, too!
Why does the rat have a wooden leg? ... Because it is a former pirate!
Why did the spider cross the road? ... Because it ... oh you already know this one!?
I had a cousin named like that.
He died some years ago.
Isn't he the author of the book 'Fun with Demons'?
He is kind of a father figure to me. Of course he denies all kinship to me.
He's a smart one. I heared he hid in a foreign country as the first bugs showed up.
I am not foolish enough to believe in the existence of this weapon.
Oh, I saw some demon carvings in the dungeons as I hid there after a little joke on old Stutch.
Yes, they showed demons, seven actually, dancing around a sword! In something like a flaming pit.
Ah, don't ask me! Usually mages and mystics know more about such stuff.
I'm the royal jes ... uhm ... the royal tax-collector! Do you want to pay your taxes?
Well, perhaps later.
I actually know some spells! Do you want to learn how to 'lessen your load' for 200 gold?
You don't know what offer you are missing!
Do you want to buy a 'mace of the fury' for 250 gold?
You dont know what offer you have passed!
Do you want to kiss me?
Pah, I didn't want to kiss you anyway!
Has any man said to you that you're not only beautiful but also intelligent?
This is a world of fantasy and full of surprises!
Well, think about it!
Of course, just browse through my wares.
You may buy all the things we grow or gather at this place.
We abandoned the gods a long time ago. A short time after they abandoned us.
They are lost, and if they still exist they are alone in the cold and the darkness.
The Kuridai left the true path and can't see their error. Their way of living may have been suitable in the past, but if they don't come back to us, their path will lead into darkness.
We have still much to learn but we are on the correct path at least.
The Cenath forgot as many as they learned. I doubt they find the wisdom they are looking for without the things they neglected in their pursuit of knowledge.
I don't claim to understand this creatures but sometimes they are more close to the roots than we are.
They are so many, so planless, so divided. They choose a path I do not want for my own race.
Life takes many forms. Plants are a very basic form of life. Its simplicity makes them close to the core of nature.
Life takes many forms. Plants are a very basic form of life. Its simplicity makes them close to the core of nature.

walkAway: Remember: If you are heavily wounded or poisoned, I can heal you for free.

responses: This is a special blessing I can bestow upon you once you have obtained at least one of the other blessings and which functions a bit differently. ...
It only works when you're killed by other adventurers, which means that at least half of the damage leading to your death was caused by others, not by monsters or the environment. ...

The {twist of fate} will not reduce the death penalty like the other blessings, but instead prevent you from losing your other blessings as well as the amulet of loss, should you wear one. It costs the same as the other blessings. ...
Would you like to receive that protection for a sacrifice of |PVPBLESSCOST| gold, child?
Fine. You are free to decline my offer.
Ah, you want to replace your adventurer's stone for 30 gold?
Sorry, you don't have enough money.
No problem.
A blessed stake? That's a <hicks> strange request. Maybe Quentin knows more, he is one of the oldest monks after all.
You aren't looking that bad. Sorry, I can't help you. But if you are looking for additional protection you should go on the {pilgrimage} of ashes or get the protection of the {twist of fate} here.

Whenever you receive a lethal wound, your vital force is damaged and there is a chance that you lose some of your equipment. With every single of the five {blessings} you have, this damage and chance of loss will be reduced.

There are five blessings available in five sacred places: the {spiritual} shielding, the spark of the {phoenix}, the {embrace} of Tibia, the fire of the {suns} and the wisdom of {solitude}. Additionally, you can receive the {twist of fate} here.
You can ask for the blessing of spiritual shielding in the whiteflower temple south of Thais.
The druids north of Carlin will provide you with the embrace of Tibia.
You can ask for the blessing of the two suns in the suntower near Ab'Dendriel.
The spark of the phoenix is given by the dwarven priests of earth and fire in Kazordoon.
Talk to the hermit Eremo on the isle of Cormaya about this blessing.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

responses: I'm the foreman of this {mine}.
We understand the ways of the earth like nobody else.
I'm a miner, not your mother. Go ask someone else.
The hideout of the Horned Fox is probably a dangerous if not lethal place for inexperienced adventurers. It is the source of all the {trouble} around here.
He is a minotaur who was kicked out of Mintwallin. He must have some kind of {hideout} nearby.
This is not an amusement park! Leave the miners and their drilling-worms alone and get out! We've already got enough {trouble} without you.
We occasionally come across nasty beasts in the deepest mines.
My name is Budrik Deepdigger, Son of Earth, from the Molten Rock.
The {Horned Fox} is leading his bandits in sneak attacks and raids on us.
Yes, I remember him well. It was a tragedy. An earthquake led to a cave-in and many of our brave miners died. ...
Their ghosts still haunt the Grothmok tunnel in which they died, so we had to seal it off.
You may enter the tunnel.
This is no funhouse. Leave the miners and their drilling-worms alone and get out! We have already enough trouble without you.
Salutations, commoner |PLAYERNAME|!
We are in constant training and in perfect health.
I am the general of the queen's army. I don't have time to explain this concept to you.
Our family has been serving the Carlin army since countless generations!
Our family has been serving the Carlin army since countless generations!
She is one of my beloved sisters and serves Carlin as a town guard.
HAIL TO QUEEN ELOISE, OUR NOBLE {LEADER}!
Queen Eloise is a fine leader for our fair town, indeed!
The army protects the defenceless males of our {city}. Our elite forces are the {Green Ferrets}.
Our city blends in with the nature surrounding it. Our {druids} take care of that.
They are our main magic support and play a major role in our battle {tactics}.
Our tactic is to kiss.
K.I.S.S.! Keep It Simple, Stupid! Complicated tactics are too easy to be crushed by a twist of fate.
Our elite forces are trained by rangers and druids. In the woods they come a close second to elves.
Join what?
Sorry, we don't recruit foreigners. Maybe you can join if you prove yourself in a mission for the queen.

I am the captain of the 'Sea Goddess', the splendid and beautiful ship you see over there.
I am the captain of the 'Sea Goddess', the splendid and beautiful ship you see over there.
The 'Sea Goddess' is the most beautiful in Oramond, a whole new model of ship, elegant, sure and swift. There's no weather she can't take!
Ah, your lands are quite a sight! I have only visited Thais so far, but I think of navigating to more cities, when the time - and public demand - comes.
I will gladly take on board everything you wish to transport. No pets, though.
<bows> I would be delighted to have you on board.

My name is Haba, Captain Haba for ya little worm.
I ain't no pirate. Don't let yourself mislead by my pirate hat. I got it from a friend of mine.
Most o' my friends are dead the others don't like me anymore......well, seems there aren't many left.
What fo' godsake shall that be??
Who are ya calling a barbarian?!? Watch your tongue here on board or you end up as fish fodder!
Dis is no passenger ship it's a WARship. I'm on hunting business.
Never heard of him. He is no captain that's for sure, otherwise I'd know him. I was on the sea for the last 20 years hunting this, this monster.
Yee, I've been chasing that monster for over 20 years now. I promise I'll bring it down!
It lets me forget the long years, the lost years. The years I was chasing after THAT monster, the sea serpent.
Ye, I command 'is ship!
Our last ship has been destroyed by the most evil creature of the ocean: the sea serpent. To continue our hunt, I've bought this ship here.
Never seen one.
I'm faithful - I'll catch that monster one day. Yes I will, as sure as my name is Captain Haba!!
On the high seas there is no queen. At least I didn't see her yet. Hararar!
On the high seas there is no king. At least I didn't see him yet. Hararar!
Arms, legs, head, whatever. I got injuries on almost every part of my body!

greeting: Hello, |PLAYERNAME|. Please leave me alone in my {mourning}.

responses: All is lost. With {Winfred} dead, my love has died and I'm only an empty shell without hope or purpose.
We put all our hope in this far away colony. Sadly, we never made it there and I will never know what our life would have been like in Port Hope.
I guess he was the one that put my father up to the whole thing. He spied on Winfred and me and it was certainly him that suggested my {imprisonment} in this tower.
This forsaken place seems as remote from the rest of the world as my heart is.
Please leave me alone in my mourning.
The bustling streets of Thais are all but a faint memory to me.
Carlin is a lovely and green city as far as I remember.
My father wanted me to marry a wealthy Venorean. He understood so little about love and the ways of the heart.
The city of the elves is an exotic wonder.
The city is like the dwarfs that built it. Stony, never-changing and hard to understand for an outsider.

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.
This is a special blessing I can bestow upon you once you have obtained at least one of the other blessings and which functions a bit differently. ...
It only works when you're killed by other adventurers, which means that at least half of the damage leading to your death was caused by others, not by monsters or the environment. ...

The {twist of fate} will not reduce the death penalty like the other blessings, but instead prevent you from losing your other blessings as well as the amulet of loss, should you wear one. It costs the same as the other blessings. ...
Would you like to receive that protection for a sacrifice of |PVPBLESSCOST| gold, child?
Fine. You are free to decline my offer.
Ah, you want to replace your adventurer's stone for 30 gold?
Sorry, you don't have enough money.
No problem.
You aren't looking that bad. Sorry, I can't help you. But if you are looking for additional protection you should go on the {pilgrimage} of ashes or get the protection of the {twist of fate} here.

Whenever you receive a lethal wound, your vital force is damaged and there is a chance that you lose some of your equipment. With every single of the five {blessings} you have, this damage and chance of loss will be reduced.

There are five blessings available in five sacred places: the {spiritual} shielding, the spark of the {phoenix}, the {embrace} of Tibia, the fire of the {suns} and the wisdom of {solitude}. Additionally, you can receive the {twist of fate} here.
You can ask for the blessing of spiritual shielding in the whiteflower temple south of Thais.
The druids north of Carlin will provide you with the embrace of Tibia.
You can ask for the blessing of the two suns in the suntower near Ab'Dendriel.
The spark of the phoenix is given by the dwarven priests of earth and fire in Kazordoon.
Talk to the hermit Eremo on the isle of Cormaya about this blessing.

greeting: At your service |PLAYERNAME| and welcome to the post office.

responses: Here. Don't forget that you need to buy a label too if you want to send a parcel. Always write the name of the {receiver} in the first line.
Of course, just browse through my wares.
My name is Cipfried.
Me? Oh, I'm just a humble {monk}. Ask me if you need {help} or {healing}.
Now it is |TIME|, my child.
I have sacrificed my life to serve the good gods of {Tibia} and to help newcomers in this world.
You can deposit your money at the bank. That way you won't lose it if you get {killed}. The bank is just under the academy and to the right.
Your near destiny will be to choose a {vocation} once you are level 8. Whether you will become a {knight}, {sorcerer}, {paladin} or {druid} is a big decision!
That's where we are - the world of Tibia, in a village called {Rookgaard}, to be precise, with many {monsters}, {quests} and {citizens} around.
How can I help you? I can tell you about specific {citizens} if you tell me their name. I can also give you general {hints} about {Tibia}.

Only a few people live in our village, most of them are {merchants}. You can either ask them for a {trade} or about various other topics, like the names of other citizens, their {job} or Tibia in general.
I don't have any goods for trade, but if you ask one of the {merchants}, he or she will gladly show you what you can buy or sell.
A single rat doesn't pose a grave danger to you, but be careful not to get cornered by many of them. They could still {kill} you.
If you face spiders, beware of the {poisonous} ones. If you are poisoned, you will constantly lose health. Come to me and I'll heal you from poison.
If you get killed, you will revive in this temple. However, you will lose experience and also equipment, which can be quite painful. Take good care of yourself!
Poison is very dangerous! Don't ever drink green liquids, they are poisonous and will make you lose health!
There are four vocations in Tibia: {knight}, {sorcerer}, {paladin} or {druid}. Each vocation has its unique special abilities.
Knights are close combat fighters. They usually wield melee weapons such as swords, axes or clubs.
Druids are nature magicians. Their speciality is casting ice and earth magic, as well as providing healing for others.
Paladins are well-trained distance fighters and can cast holy magic. You will usually see them wearing bows or crossbows.
Sorcerers are elemental magicians. They have mastered fire, energy and death magic.
You can buy and sell goods from merchants. To do so, simply talk to them and ask them for a {trade}. They will gladly show you their offers and also the things they buy from you.
You can buy equipment from the {merchants} in this village once you earned some {gold}. Some {monsters} also carry equipment pieces with them.
Shovels are very useful. There may be a hidden entrance under a loose pile of stone and without a shovel, how would you know?
Never go adventuring without a rope! If you don't have one when you fall through a hole in the ground, you won't be able to leave without a rope.
To defend yourself against enemies, you will need a weapon. You can purchase weapons at {Obi}'s shop.
Food is very important for your health. If you are hurt in a fight with a {monster}, select 'Use' on food such as cheese, ham or meat to eat it. This will slowly refill your health.
As a premium adventurer, you are an official tax payer and thus privileged in many ways. For example, you can travel by {ship} and get better deals from {merchants}.
Ships are a comfortable way of travelling to distant cities. At any harbour, you can board the ship and ask its captain where he sails to.
Travelling by ship will cost you some gold, though, so be sure to have money with you.
Potions will come in handy once you are in a fight deep in a dungeon. If you aren't in immediate danger, you can simply eat {food} to regain health, though.
The academy is just north of here. You can learn helpful things there from {Seymour}, if you wish to!
Well, King Tibianus of course. The island of {Rookgaard} belongs to his kingdom.
The {gods} have chosen this isle as the point of arrival for newborn souls. The {citizens} will help you if you ask them.
They created {Tibia} and all life on it. Visit our {academy} and learn about them.
Monsters are a constant threat to this village. You would tremendously help us by fighting them, starting with {rats} in the {sewers}, then turning to {spiders} or {wolves}.
You can buy and sell goods from merchants. To do so, simply talk to them and ask them for a {trade}. They will gladly show you their offers and also the things they buy from you.
The sewers are right below this village. North of this temple, you find a sewer grate which leads down, but there are also many small huts in the village which are connected to the sewers.
I'm sorry, but I'm not interested in worldly goods. If you want to buy or sell something, you should ask a local merchant for a trade.
To defend yourself against enemies, you will need armor and shields. You can purchase both at {Dixi}'s shop.
You can earn a fair amount of gold by fighting {monsters} and picking up the things they carry. Many {citizens} are buying some of that loot, simply ask around.
Oh, I can handle my tasks myself, thank you. If you are looking for something to do, you should listen to the local {citizens} and ask them for {quests}. You could also help us fight {monsters}.
Wolves can only be found outside of the village. If you want to know where their dens are, best talk to {Dallheim} or {Zerbrus} at the bridges.
I can see a bright future for you... you will soon embark on a very big adventure and explore the world of {Tibia} - maybe even influence history!
You aren't looking really bad, |PLAYERNAME|. I can only help in cases of real emergencies. Raise your health simply by eating {food}.
Obi's {shop} is to the north-east of this humble temple. He sells {weapons}, and his granddaughter {Dixi} sells {armor} and {shields} upstairs.
She recently opened a little bar north of this village.
That's a sad story. He used to help me guard this temple, but for some reason he went out of his mind.
I absolutely appreciate what he's doing. He has helped me a lot since he took up the job of welcoming newcomers to this island.
That old lady hasn't had an easy life. Her son {Tom} completely abandoned her, but I have no idea why.
In Al Dee's shop you'll find important general equipment such as {ropes}, {shovels} and torches.
Amber is such a lovely girl, and also a very experienced {explorer} and {adventurer}. It's always interesting to chat with her.
He's a farmer, just like his cousin {Willie}. He doesn't talk to everybody, though.
Willie is a farmer, just like his cousin {Billy}. His farm is located left of the temple. You can buy and sell {food} there.
Yes? How can I {help} you?
That's {Obi's} granddaughter. Her counter is just upstairs from Obi's shop. She helps him by selling {armor} and {shields}.
Hyacinth is a close friend of mine. He left the village many years ago to live in solitude. I think all this bustle here simply was too much for him.
Lee'Delle's shop is on the {premium} side of the village. You should visit her!
You must have passed the house of Lily on your way here. She sells {potions}. If you pick blueberries or find cookies, she'll buy them from you.
You can find the oracle on the top floor of the {academy}, just above {Seymour}. Go there when you are level 8 to find your {destiny}.
He is the {bank} clerk, making sure your {money} remains safe.
Seymour is a loyal follower of the {king} and a teacher in the {academy}.
Tom is the main provider for leather armor in this village. You can sell fresh corpses or even minotaur leather to him.
May the gods bless our loyal guardsmen! Day and night they stand watch on our bridges, ensuring that it is not passed by dangerous {monsters}!

greeting: Blessings, Player! How may I be of service? Do you wish to trade some {token}s, or would you like some {information} or {talk}? Should I {enchant} certain items for you?

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Welcome to the finest {armor} shop in the land, |PLAYERNAME|!

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.
I run this armoury. If you want to protect your life, you better buy my wares.

greeting: Welcome on board, |PLAYERNAME|. Where can I {sail} you today, to {Grimvale} or {Edron}?
farewell: Good bye. Recommend us if you were satisfied with our service.

responses: I can travel you to {Grimvale} or {Edron}.

greeting: Welcome, |PLAYERNAME|! Feel free to tell me what has brought you here.

responses: I can offer you first & second addons of the following outfit: {Wayfarer}.
Ask about {first addon} or {second addon}.
Ask about {first addon} or {second addon}.
Ask about {first addon} or {second addon}.
llected all the required pieces, say 'yes' and voila - you got yourself an addon!
To achieve the first storage addon you need to give me the Old Cape. Do you have them with you?
Alright then. Come back when you got all neccessary items.
To achieve the second storage addon you need to give me the Sedge Hat. Do you have them with you?
Alright then. Come back when you got all neccessary items.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Be greeted, traveller |PLAYERNAME|. Welcome to my {hut}.

responses: Do you seek a passage to the isle of the kings for 10 gold coins?
Well, I'll be here if you change your mind.
My name is Dalbrect Windtrouser, of the once proud Windtrouser family.
I am merely a humble fisher now that nothing is left of my noble {legacy}.
Once my family was once noble and wealthy, but {fate} turned against us and threw us into poverty.
When Carlin tried to colonize the region now known as the ghostlands, my ancestors put their fortune in that {project}.
When Carlin tried to colonize the region now known as the ghostlands, my ancestors put their fortune in that {project}.
Our family fortune was lost when the colonization of those cursed lands failed. Now nothing is left of our fame or our fortune. If I only had something as a reminder of those better times. <sigh>
Our family fortune was lost when the colonization of those cursed lands failed. Now nothing is left of our fame or our fortune. If I only had something as a reminder of those better times. <sigh>
To think my family used to belong to the local nobility! And now those arrogant women are in charge!
There is little I can offer you but a trip with my boat. Are you looking for a {passage} to the isle of kings perhaps?
My ship is my only pride and joy.

greeting: Greetings, |PLAYERNAME|! You're looking really bad. Let me heal your wounds.
<nods> At your service, |PLAYERNAME|, protecting the {village} from {monsters}.

responses: Dallheim, at your service.
I'm the guard of this bridge. I defend Rookgaard against the beasts of the {wilderness} and the {dungeons}, and even of the stinking {sewers}.
My duty is eternal. Time is of no importance.
Before you fight monsters, deposit your money there. If you die, you won't lose it if it's save in the bank.
Fine.
I have to stay here, sorry. But I can heal you if you are wounded. I have also valuable information about {monsters}.
I'm an expert concerning the wildlife and {monsters} on this island.
I'll protect all citizens alike. That is my {job} and duty.
Did you know that rats are among the most dangerous animals in whole Tibia? Each day, they kill many people who are overestimating their powers. Don't be one of them!
There are two types of spiders on this island: normal spiders and poison spiders. As their name says, the latter ones can poison you which will cause a constant loss of your health. Beware of them!
Once you have upgraded your starter {equipment} a little - maybe leather stuff - and got a better weapon as well as a shield, you should be able to kill trolls.
You need a stronger weapon than the starter club to kill wolves. Maybe a {sabre} or a short {sword} will do.
Orcs are a constant menace. There are even stronger species of orcs on the main continent.
Minotaurs are very strong. You will need a group of people or at least level 6 and good equipment to be able to kill them.
They are a little tougher than they look like, but they're still good beginner monsters.
The skeletons on this island are usually fairly hidden. They are often found near graves and always underground. Beware of their evil life drain.
A bear can easily kill you if you don't pay attention.
Wasps are the fastest monsters on this island and they poison you. If you ever run into one and are already low of health, fear for your life!
Snakes can poison you, too, but their poison is usually not as dangerous as that of poison spiders. Just be careful and kill them fast.
Premium warriors pay. They gain many advantages this way.
HAIL TO THE KING!
All that theory won't help you without some real battle practice.
A place of protection. Nothing can harm you there.
I can mark you some dungeons on your map if you ask me about {monsters} suited for your level.
The sewers are crowded with {rats}. They make fine targets for young heroes.
There are {wolves}, {bears}, {snakes}, {deer}, and {spiders} in the wilderness past this bridge.
I'm a follower of {Banor}.
The heavenly warrior! Hail to him!
I'm not interested in such party tricks.
A nice place for a hero, but nothing for whelps.
It's my duty to protect this village and its {citizens}.
It looks like you have mastered the drill.
Sorry, I don't trade. Ask the shop owners for a trade instead.
I don't approve of her opening a bar. Those drunken adventurers stumbling over the bridge at nighttime are not especially helpful in improving the situation.
Zerbrus tells me such strange stories about him. Well, he sees him all day from the west bridge.
He dedicated his life to welcoming newcomers to this island.
Nice old lady.
He has been on this island as long as me.
I don't trust her.
He guards the temple and can heal you if you are badly injured or poisoned, just like me.
She's {Obi's} granddaughter and deals with {armors} and {shields}. Her shop is south west of town, close to the temple.
Strange fellow, hides somewhere in the mountains of the isle.
Her shop is on the {premium} side of town. Best offers around.
Sometimes I wish I had an easy job like her, selling {potions} all day.
You can find the oracle on the top floor of the {academy}, just above Seymour. Go there when you are level 8.
The local bank clerk.
Leave me alone with this wimp.
He's the local tanner. You could try selling fresh corpses or leather to him.
At your service.
He's guarding the west bridge. Not that a real threat would lurk on the other side.
He is a fine cook and farmer. He buys and sells {food}.
You aren't looking really bad. Eat some food to regain strength.

farewell: May your path always be even.

farewell: Please come back from time to time.

It's pretty, isn't it?
I am Dane.
It is exactly |TIME|.
I can offer you milk, water, and lemonade.
Alcohol makes people too aggressive. We don't need such stuff in Carlin.
Alcohol makes people too aggressive. We don't need such stuff in Carlin.

greeting: Greetings seekers of knowledge. You seem to be just the person I'm {looking} for.

responses: I need the help of some competent {adventurers} to handle a threat to all creation.
This leaves us with no choice but to take action into our own {hands}.
I guess you know about the {background} and there is no need to tell you that the forces from beyond managed to acquire the parts of the godbreaker in a coup.
The secret locations of the godbreaker {parts} were revealed and due to trickery, the minions of Variphor aquired all of them.
Those pieces of knowledge come in several forms and shapes. For most I can give you more or less specific hints where to start your {search}.
You have to {find} the veiled hoard of Zathroth, breach it and destroy the knowledge how to use the godbreaker.
Of course the first to ask would be the famous Avar Tar, but I heard he's already on a quest of his own and ...
Well, let's say our last collaboration did not end too well. In fact, I'd be not even surprised if he pretended to not even know me. ...
So I have to look elsewhere to handle this new {threat}.
The goodbreaker was created in ancient times, when the war between the gods and their minions was on its height. Its creation took aeons and incredible sacrifices. ...
Each part had to be crafted perfectly, to emulate the gods, so it would share 'the same place' with them. ...
Mere mortals can not even perceive it in his whole but only recognize the part of it that is the physical representation in our world. ...

If it was meant to be used as an actual weapon, as the ultimate threat, or if Zathroth was just tempted to use his knowledge in the ultimate way - to create something that could undo himself - we don't know. ...
However in the end even Zathroth deemed it too much of a threat but instead of destroying the contraption once and for all, it was {disassembled} and hidden away.
The parts alone do them no good. To assemble the parts, great skill, immense power and forbidden knowledge are necessary. ...
The skill will be supplied by the fallen Yalahari and the power by Variphor itself. ...
The only thing they are still lacking is the knowledge to assemble and operate the {godbreaker}.
The godbreaker is a complex artifact. Incantation woven into incantation. The powers bound into it are so immense that the slightest mishandling could prove disastrous. ...
o figure out how it works, let alone how it can be operated safely, could require several centuries of tireless study. And even then this information would be only partial. ...
Yet the creation and operation of the godbreaker is just the kind of forbidden {knowledge} Zathroth values most, so it was compiled and stored.
Of course the dangers of such knowledge were obvious. It was hidden in a sacred place devoted to Zathroth and dangerous knowledge. ...
The hidden library, the forbidden hoard, the shrouded trove of knowledge or the veiled hoard of forbidden knowledge, the place has many names in many {myths}.
The myths agree that the place is well hidden, extremely guarded and contains some of the most powerful pieces of knowledge in this world and probably beyond. ...
However the knowledge about the godbreaker now poses a threat to all existence. In the hands of Variphor it can cause disaster in previously unknown ways. The gods themselves are in {peril}.
Regardless of the dangers, the cult of Zathroth refused to destroy the knowledge of the godbreaker for good. ...
They {value} dangerous knowledge that much, that they are unable to part from it, even when faced with the utter destruction of creation.
I know it's asked much but it's no longer a matter of choice. ...
The enemy is moving and I have reports that suggest the minions of Variphor are actively searching for Zathroth's library. They must not be allowed to succeed. ...
We must be the first to {reach} the hoard and make sure the enemy doesn't get the information he needs.
I'd recommend to follow the few leads me and my associates could gather so far. ...
Old myths, some {rumors} about old texts and other pieces of knowledge that I could use to figure out where to locate the hidden library and how to enter it.
Hints about the shrouded hoard are numerous, though most are general mentions in texts that deal with Zathroth. But there are other sources. ...
Like texts about ancient liturgies of Zathroth and historical documents that might give us clues. I already compiled everything of value from the sources that were openly available. ...
To gather the more {obscure} parts of knowledge, however, I'll need your help.

greeting: Hello, traveller |PLAYERNAME|. How can I help you?

responses: I am the magistrate of this isle.
Thats me.
I am Dermot, the magistrate of this isle.
Time is not important on Fibula.
You are at Fibula. This isle is not very dangerous. Just the wolves bother outside the village.
Oh, my god. In the dungeon of Fibula are a lot of monsters. That's why we have sealed it with a solid door.
Oh, my god. In the dungeon of Fibula are a lot of monsters. That's why we have sealed it with a solid door.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Good day, Ma'am. How may I help you, |PLAYERNAME|? If you like to see my offers, simply ask me for a trade!
Good day, Sir. How may I help you, |PLAYERNAME|? If you like to see my offers, simply ask me for a trade!

responses: Here, take a look and choose something nice for you!
I'm Dixi.
<giggles> I love gossiping - err, I mean providing valuable information of course, just tell me a name! <winks>
I'm helping my grandfather {Obi} with this shop. If you like to see my offers, simply ask me for a {trade}!
It's |TIME|.
You can deposit your {money} there to keep it safe. However, you still need cash to buy something here.
The Island of Destiny can be reached via the {oracle} once you are level 8. This trip will help you choose your {profession}!
You will learn everything you need to know about professions once you reach the {Island of Destiny}.
As a premium adventurer you can visit different regions on this island, trade with {Lee'Delle} and much more.
As an adventurer, you should always have at least a {backpack}, a {rope}, a {shovel}, a {weapon}, an {armor} and a {shield}.
If you need a torch, go see {Al Dee}'s store.
Oh, I'm sorry, but my grandfather {Obi} doesn't like seeing me waving around with pointy things. He sells weapons himself, just go downstairs!
Are you hungry? Then you should visit {Willie} or {Billy} in the West of town. {Norma} also has yummy stuff! Oh, and you could pick {blueberries} or eat the food some {monsters} carry with them.
{Lily} sells effective health potions and antidote potions against poison.
The big building in the centre of Rookgaard. They have a library, a training centre, a {bank} and the room of the {oracle}. {Seymore} is the teacher there.
King Tibianus is a wise and noble monarch. This island of {Rookgaard} belongs to his kingdom.
Our lovely village will provide shelter for you. Once you are level 8, you can travel to {mainland} and choose a {profession}.
If you want to fight monsters in {dungeons}, you definitely need some good {equipment} such as a {weapon}, an {armor} and a {shield}.
Just ask me for a {trade} to check out my offers.
Just ask me for a {trade} to see what I buy from you.
To view the offers of a merchant, simply talk to him or her and ask for a {trade}.
There are many blueberry bushes in and around this village. Just use them to your liking!
You need to make sure you have some basic {equipment} before you head into dungeons such as the {sewers}.
There is a sewer grate which you can use to enter the sewers just left of this building. Eww, this smell!
I'm fine, thank you.
How can I help you? I can give you general {hints}, information about {citizens} or talk about many other topics. Just ask me!
I'm sorry honey, but I can't lend you gold. Earn it yourself by fighting {monsters} and picking up the things they carry. Sell it to {merchants} to make profit!
You should never embark on an adventure without it. Ask {Al Dee} or {Lee'Delle} for it!
Yes, I can sell that to you. Just ask me for a {trade} to see what I buy from you.
Me and my grandfather {Obi} sell {equipment} of all kinds. Just ask him or me for a {trade} if you like to see our offers.
Norma used to sell {equipment}, but she finally earned enough {money} to fulfil one of her dreams - her own bar!
I never understand what he's talking about.
I'm glad that he's here. He does a great job!
Sometimes, I help Zirella collecting wood. Her old back aches all the time. It's a shame her son Tom doesn't help her.
In Al Dee's shop you will find important general equipment such as {ropes}, {shovels} and torches.
One day, I want to become a brave adventurer like her and leave this town!
Billy can be kind of scary. He swears a lot and doesn't talk to everybody.
Uncle Willie and {Billy} are farmers, which means they are a good place to sell and buy {food}.
He can heal you if you're injured or poisoned.
Did I hear my name somewhere?
I think he's a bit strange, living alone outside this village. How can he dislike to chat with everybody here?
Lee'Delle's shop is on the {premium} side of the village. You should visit it!
I always give my cookies and blueberries to Lily. She loves them! Also, she sells {potions}.
You can find the oracle on the top floor of the {academy}, just above {Seymour}. Go there when you are level 8 to find your {destiny}.
He is the local {bank} clerk.
Seymour is a loyal follower of the {king} and a teacher in the {academy}.
I wish he would talk to his mother {Zirella} again. I feel so sorry for her. Anyway, as Tom is a tanner, you can sell leather and fresh corpses to him.
He's one of our reliable guardsmen. Without them, many {monsters} would roam the village of {Rookgaard}!

greeting: This world is nothing without poetry, don't you think? It gives us hope, it envokes love. It's inciting and invigorating all the same.

responses: Our world, our home, our very own plane of existence. We have to protect it, mind you. Underneath all the battles, challenges and monsters there still resides a majestic, yet vulnerable being. A mother to us all.
A king and a beggar know more than a king alone... ahem, except for King Tibianus. He most certainly would. All hail King Tibianus etc. etc.
So, you're on a mission. Aren't we all? A quest to savour the important moments, the valuable memories? To fight for love and happiness, heroically and against all odds? Yes, we are indeed.
So, you're on a mission. Aren't we all? A quest to savour the important moments, the valuable memories? To fight for love and happiness, heroically and against all odds? Yes, we are indeed.
Rather a calling. The weight of words is something not easily lifted by some, yet it can be a mighty weapon to others. ...
