I am the leader of the Paladins. I help our members.
Paladins are great warriors and magicians. Besides that we are excellent missile fighters. Many people in Tibia want to join us.
Of course, we aren't as strong as knights, but no druid or sorcerer will ever defeat a paladin with a sword.
There are many magic spells and runes paladins can use.
Paladins are the best missile fighters in Tibia!
I am a paladin, not a storyteller.
Every paladin profits from his vocation. It has many advantages to be a paladin.
We will help you to improve your skills. Besides I offer spells for paladins.
Harkath Bloodblade is the royal general.
Some paladins serve in the kings army.
He has some potential.
How spineless do you have to be to become a jester?
The McRonalds are simple farmers.
He must have been skilled before he became the way he is now. Such a pity.
Yes?
The alcohol he sells shrouds the mind and the eye.
One of the most important members of our guild. She makes all the bows and arrows we need.
He sells a lot of useful equipment.
He and his guildfellows lack the grace of a true warrior.
A fine warrior and a skilled general.
King Tibianus is a wise ruler.
A follower of evil that will get what he deserves one day.
Mhm, a little too nice for my taste. Still, it's amazing how she endures all those men stalking her, especially this creepy Oswald.
A skilled healer, that's for sure.
Just another arrogant sorcerer.
If there wouldn't be higher powers to protect him...
A humble monk and a wise man.
Strong man. But a little shy.

greeting: Be greeted |PLAYERNAME|. What is your {business} near the {hellgate}?

responses: I am the leader of the Kuridai and the Az'irel of Ab'Dendriel. Humans would call it {sheriff}, executioner, or avenger.
Sometimes people get imprisoned for some time. True criminals will be cast out and for committing the worst crimes offenders are thrown into the hellgate.
Sometimes people get imprisoned for some time. True criminals will be cast out and for committing the worst crimes offenders are thrown into the hellgate.
Sometimes people get imprisoned for some time. True criminals will be cast out and for committing the worst crimes offenders are thrown into the hellgate.
It was here among other structures, like the depot tower, before our people came here. It's secured by a sealed door.
For safety we keep the door to the hellgate locked all times. I have the {keys} to open it when needed.
For safety we keep the door to the hellgate locked all times. I have the {keys} to open it when needed.
Not that I like to talk to you, but I am Elathriel Shadowslayer.
It's one of the more useful concepts we can learn from the other races.
It's hard for some of my people to grasp the true concept of a strong leader.
I mastered some battle spells.
I couldn't care less.
A human weakling, not much more.
A human weakling, not much more.
Druidic magic is too peaceful for my taste.
I have seen human sorcerers doing some impressive things ... before they died.
We might use the shelter earth and hills provide us, but their obsession for metal is a waste of time.
Like all inferior races, they can be at least of use for something good. The other castes are just jealous of us making use of them.
I still doubt it exists.
Even if he'd walk through the town above, the other castes would not see the necessity to follow OUR way.
My people are divided in castes in these times, until they comprehend that only the way of the Kuridai can save us all.
Arrogant bastards, but they wield quite powerful magic.
Dreamers are of no practical use. I don't mourn their demise.
Confused cowards. For all their skill, they still tend to hide and run. What a waste.
We are the heart of the elven society. We forge, we build, and we don't allow our people to be pushed around.
We watch this city and the actions of its inhabitants closely.
The merchants of venore provide us with some useful goods. Still I an convinced that they get more out of our bargain then we do.
The Thaian kingdom and we share some enemies, so it's only logical to cooperate in a few areas.
I offer hardly anything except for knowledge of {spells}.
I offer hardly anything except for knowledge of {spells}.
I offer hardly anything except for knowledge of {spells}.
The elves of this city are the casts of the Cenath, the Kuridai, and the Deraisim.
I am a guardian of this town. I have no time to chat!
The Cenath are magic users. Look for them on the upper levels of the town.
The Deraisim are scouts and hunters. You may find them on the groundlevel of the city.
There are no Teshial.
If you are looking for that kind of equipment you should ask a Kuridai.
Ask around in Ab'Dendriel. Many elves can teach you something about magic. The Cenath love magic most of all.
The city of the humans lies somewhere far to the south beyond the mountains of the dwarfs.

I sell musical instruments of many kinds.
I sell lyres, lutes, drums, and simple fanfares.
Music is an attempt to condensate emotions in harmonies and save them for the times to come.
Time has its own song. Close your eyes and listen to the symphony of the seasons.
Everything is a song. Life, death, history ... everything. To listen to the song of something is the first step to understand it.
Everything is a song. Life, death, history ... everything. To listen to the song of something is the first step to understand it.
We are the most graceful of all races. We feel the music of the universe in our hearts and souls.
They could dig some halls for a big musical event, but they won't listen to me about that matter.
I bet they were great musicians.
That is some god the humans worship. Our pople are not interested in this gods anymore.
They are too loud and don't even understand the concept of a melody.
The other deraisim are too much concerned with mastering the nature so they don't listen to its music anymore.
The Cenath think they know the 'art' but the only true art is the music.
I went down to the mines and tried to lighten up their spirit, the foolish creatures did not listen to my songs, though.
Sorry, I don't feel like teaching magic today.

For the worst of crimes, criminals are cast into hellgate. It is said no one can return from there. Since it is not actually forbidden to enter hellgate, you might convince Elathriel to grant you entrance.

greeting: A shadow preceded you. You wish a {scroll} or a {mission}?
Hello! Ever asked yourself who killed all the monsters for the wall trophies? Yeah, that was me, Emael the Beasthunter! I am an expert in displaying trophies. ...
So if you have at least some dangerous monster to show off I strongly advise you to aquire a {podium} of vigour.
farewell: I wish you a good hunt. Goodbye!
LONG LIVE THE QUEEN! You may leave now!

responses: I am privileged to be in service of our beloved queen!
We are proud people who fought hard for their freedom and won't allow others to oppress us again.
I am the head of Carlin's Girls Brigade, also known as CGB.
The CGB is a patriotic organisation that fights our numerous enemies with means that guards or army do not have at their disposal. ...
We work secretly and covertly. We uncover secret plots and we are both the first line of defence of our city and the last. We are joined only by the best of the best.
The AVIN is rather a crime syndicate than anything else. If there is something dirty and illegal, they are most likely involved. ...
They are unscrupulous and also do not back away from blackmailing and assassination.
The TBI is as old-fashioned, stubborn and inflexible as only males can be. What makes this bureaucracy somewhat dangerous, is the money they have at their disposal. ...
They buy spies and traitors - all of them weak-willed or greedy individuals.
I am the head of Carlin's Girls Brigade, also known as CGB.
I am known as Emma.
The elves of Ab'Dendriel are our allies. Our druids contribute most to keeping such a good relation. ...
They seem to understand the elves a bit better than we ordinary people do.
The Thaians never got over the fact that we gained our independence. They do everything they can to hinder the prospering of our city.
Venore is a hellhole of evil. The trade barons' greed lets them plot against all other cities and even against each other.
Svargrond is an important project for our city. It's not a colony but many of our people live there alongside those barbarians. If everything works out fine, Carlin and Svargrond will both prosper.
Strange people with strange customs live in this city. Luckily, we rarely have to deal with them.
Darashia never bothers about the affairs of other cities.
The name of the city is a cruel joke. The people there are oppressed by Thais and Venore who slowly bleed the isle and its people white. ...
It shows what would have happened to us if our rebellion had failed.
It's just another Thaian puppet. Still, it is distant enough to stand a fair chance to get rid of Thais's oppression one day.
The dwarfs of Kazordoon usually mind their own business. I wish all other cities would do the same.

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.
I'm professor Falonzo from the mage's guild.
I'm a researcher of scientific council of the mage guild and I came to that {place} to study this {anomaly}.
This {plane} is now inhabited by {intruders} and creatures that accidentally became {dragged} in. ...
It is neither completely of our world nor is it still that disconnected and unreachable as it used to be. I fear it's only a harbinger of something more dangerous and more {sinister}.
Well, the whole place here is an anomaly so to say. You can hardly have missed the fact that you arrived here through a mystical gate. ...
Well actually it's no gate at all but a rift in the fabric of nature. It is this minor {plane} trying to reconnect to our world.

This is a lesser plane of tarnished, elemental fire that once belonged to the world that we know. It was despoiled in the wars of the gods and broke loose from our world. {Lost} and drifting through the void, without a connection to our plane.
Infernalists were the first who found their way into this sphere. Hoping to tap its rotten power somehow. Yet this sphere created creatures of its own and draw others here from other planes.
This place became a fiery trap to certain beings with an affinity to fire. Somehow it reconnects randomly with the known world, to which it once belonged ...
but also to other places that it shares some affinity with like hellish places of unspeakable evil that spawn infernal creatures.
Something is tearing at the fabric of reality. I can't tell what is it but the {boundaries} between worlds are fading. ...
A process that what watched for over a century but which has extremely grown in momentum over the last few years. Something is happening and it's for sure nothing good. ...
Be it as it may, the plane trying to reconnect was only a side effect. It still might teach us about what is happening and it has for sure attracted some {attention} already.
Well, that was how it used to be. Lost and without any connection to our world. But that was before things ... have {changed}.
We know about other planes of existence but in all history it has never been as easy to reach them as it is now. ...
Sometimes world seem to overlap and we can identify more and more such planes and worlds. More then we ever had imagined. All we can tell is, that something is changing. And not for the good.
Such an anomaly can't go unnoticed for long. That lead to the presence to unwanted {intruders} and other entities were {dragged} into by the nature of the sphere.

responses: This is a special blessing I can bestow upon you once you have obtained at least one of the other blessings and which functions a bit differently. ...
It only works when you're killed by other adventurers, which means that at least half of the damage leading to your death was caused by others, not by monsters or the environment. ...

The {twist of fate} will not reduce the death penalty like the other blessings, but instead prevent you from losing your other blessings as well as the amulet of loss, should you wear one. It costs the same as the other blessings. ...
Would you like to receive that protection for a sacrifice of |PVPBLESSCOST| gold, child?
Fine. You are free to decline my offer.
Ah, you want to replace your adventurer's stone for 30 gold?
Sorry, you don't have enough money.
No problem.
You aren't looking that bad. Sorry, I can't help you. But if you are looking for additional protection you should go on the {pilgrimage} of ashes or get the protection of the {twist of fate} here.

Whenever you receive a lethal wound, your vital force is damaged and there is a chance that you lose some of your equipment. With every single of the five {blessings} you have, this damage and chance of loss will be reduced.

There are five blessings available in five sacred places: the {spiritual} shielding, the spark of the {phoenix}, the {embrace} of Tibia, the fire of the {suns} and the wisdom of {solitude}. Additionally, you can receive the {twist of fate} here.
You can ask for the blessing of spiritual shielding in the whiteflower temple south of Thais.
The druids north of Carlin will provide you with the embrace of Tibia.
You can ask for the blessing of the two suns in the suntower near Ab'Dendriel.
The spark of the phoenix is given by the dwarven priests of earth and fire in Kazordoon.
Talk to the hermit Eremo on the isle of Cormaya about this blessing.

I heard it when I was a child.

Nothing more than a tale for warriors.
Uhm, do not mention him. It may scare customers away.

Oh, the Royal Archives are in Rain Castle!
The king's residence has been renovated lately.
Many travellers tell funny stories about all the emancipated women in this northern town.
Here in Thais is the center of Tibia.
He is a little shy. In his youth he dreamed to become a druid.
He's able to drink a bottle or two.
I am trying to hire him for an evening or two.
Oh, that old guy! He's a good customer, that's for sure.
Can you believe that she actually told her guildfellows that alcohol is a bad thing?
She makes excellent arrows and bows.
Many of his customers visit my Hut, too.
The knights have sometimes parties here after some arena fights.
Too disciplined to enjoy life.
I think some time ago a stranger from Fibula with that name stayed here for some nights.
I overheard some conversations about his evilness. That's enough to hope, that I never ever meet him.
A sorceress, you can find her in their guild sitting befor a book - always!
Just between you and me: What a babe!
Marvik seldom leaves his guildhall at all.
The McRonalds are a nice couple. Donald is a dear friend of mine.
Muriel has never visited this place.
I hate him. Each of his visits here ends with a bar brawl.
He hardly visits my humble tavern.
A loud neighbour, I get a lot of complaints about him.
That fellow is filthy rich. He rented a room upstairs for months in advance and always orders the best beer and wine i serve.
I don't know where he gets these fluids. If I could sell them here, the hut would be crowded.

I am Garamond Starstream.
Did you not listen? I am the teacher of druid and sorcerer spells for level 8 to 18. I teach young adventurers spells they can use once they have the proper vocation - druid or sorcerer.
I have an old friend there. Haven't heard from him in a while.
Oh, it's not too bad here, believe me. At least I always get young and enthusiast disciples! Though I must confess I miss the vastness of the Tibian plains. <sighs>
A kind old hunter. He loves to see young life around, taking on the ways and lays of the land. If you have any question, ask Inigo for help.
Roughened and toughened by life's tragedies. A good man, but somber.
Yes, child. If you wish to learn a spell, tell me.
A very headstrong young man, though I appreciate his devotion to the craft of potion-making. No respect for senior authority or age at all! Except maybe for a little soft spot for Mr Morris.
A strange young man. He seems driven, to my mind. By what force, I do not know. I take it the world needs adventurers such as him.
Redeeming one's soul by becoming a clerk? Not very likely. Once a pirate, always a pirate. But he's a charming old rogue.
Not a half bad cook, truly. Must have been that squirrel diet, it seems to have lead him to discover a new cuisine - everything to forget the bad taste of squirrels, he said.
He is proficient in the martial arts. A very skilled teacher of spells for knights and paladins. If that is your vocation, you should talk to Ser Tybald.
Ah, yes. Travelled with Plunderpurse a lot as I recall. Captain Plunderpurse, then. Got his head full of numbers and statistics, that boy.
Each vocation has their own unique spells to enhance their fighting or defense, which none of the other vocations can use, and which you can only learn at a spell trainer of your vocation.
Most of the major cities are on the Tibian mainland, such as the rich merchant city of Venore, for example. You will find spell teachers for your vocation in almost every major city.

Tibia is the world we live in. Rookgaard is not far off from the Tibian {mainland}, though it's a tricky passage with contrary winds. On the mainland, you will find more adventure, mystery and monsters and can prove yourself a hero or villain.

A very intelligent girl. Prefers to listen to wild animals' noises instead of humans', which is quite understandable when you think about it. However, she's also a very apt healer and can give you advice on your choice of vocation.

Your choice of vocation will determine your life in Tibia, and the skills and fighting techniques you may use. There are four vocation: knight, druid, paladin and sorcerer. If you want to know more about them, talk to Oressa in the temple. I myself teach try-out spells for both the magical classes, whereas Tybald in the next room specialises in knight and paladin spells.

Very young and sometimes precipitate, burning for everything mystical, holy, godly, which is not a bad thing as such. Just a little hasty and prone to fall into a nest of monsters now and then, but he always comes out alive.
How should I know where that key has gotten to? I rarely sleep at my age anyway! Though I must say that my young adventurer fellows handled it quite carelessly, from what I saw.

greeting: Have you come to answer Gelidrazah's questions?

responses: This place is a sanctuary of Crunor and provides me with a opportunity of spiritual contemplation.
I'm a member of a circle of persons, that joined wisdom and resources for a common purpose. Let's say, we have an eye on the greater picture in the matters of our world. ...
We are watching and evaluating what is happening in our world. Trying to avert the worst and offering a helping hand where we deem it needed. ...
We usually avoid to interfere directly in the affairs of the world and vain politics are not our concern at all.
We focus our interest on this we see as threatening for live and the laws of nature itself.
Well, while I focus more on the matters of life, some of my peers have different approaches and emphasize other aspects of the world more in their observations. ...
Regardless we share a common goal of balance and harmony.
We might not be many but we are diverse. Our rather informal order came together in the dawn of time, when the wars of the gods ravaged the world.
Even we know the individual that was somewhat of our funder, only as the wise man. ...
He was the first to bring bright and dedicated minds together, to bring at least a little order and guidance into troubled and chaotic times. ...
The order predates mankind and never bothered to give itself a name. Such assumptions of pretence and vanity have no place in our mindset.
Most times we are silent watchers and keeper of knowledge that share what they have learned with each other. We are more concerned about knowledge and wisdom and power means little to us. ...
To solve problems we usually try to convince the right people to do the right thing. We usually even lack the means for a more direct interference.
Sometimes it's necessary to do something about a situation that became threatening to the world itself. ...
It is gladly a rare occurrence and usually it is sufficient to somewhat offer a guiding hand to avert a course that would lead to more dire consequences. Nonetheless sometimes we have to interfere.
Interference comes in different forms. In this particular case there is sadly little time for subtlety and a more direct approach is necessary.
The fae granted me permission to enter their hidden realm. As a druid I'm in close touch with nature so I could gain their trust. The nature spirits are inhabiting this peninsula for ages.

The fae vary greatly in size and appearance. There are different kinds of fae like fauns, pixies, pookas, swan maidens, nymphs and boogies. Those mystical creatures are wielding power in magic and elementals. ...

Most of them are rather reclusive and live peaceful lives in their secret realm. Sometimes they are called the 'children of dreams' or 'the dream born' because the fae are born from the mortals' dreams.
Fauns are half-human, half-beast nature spirits inhabiting the woods and mountains of Feyrist. They are a slightly roguish but cheerful folk, lovers of wine and dancing. ...

Fauns show a youthful and graceful aspect but they are also brave and fearless when it comes to defend themselves. As Maelyrra told me, they emerge from mortals' dreams about celebrations, music and dancing. ...
Lately, some fauns on Feyrist are tainted by the mysterious, sinister force that is threatening Feyrist as well as the rest of Tibia.
Pixies are small nature spirits and mythical creatures inhabiting the forests and plains of Feyrist. They are generally benign, but at times, they may also display mischievous traits. ...

Like most of the fae, pixies love dancing and are often gathering in larger groups to dance on secluded glades. Pixies love flowers, butterflies, shimmering beetles, gems and other colourful things. ...
They also love the taste of honey, sweetened oat and ripe grapes. As Maelyrra told me, pixies emerge from mortals' dreams about friends and family.

Pookas are nature spirits in animal form, looking like big hares with a faintly glittering fur. They are benign but mischievous, for sure with good reason regarded as the tricksters among the fae. ...

Pookas love to play pranks on others, snitching and hiding things or telling made-up stories. They are capricious and fickle creatures. Pookas emerge from mortals' dreams about gems, treasures and gold. ...
Lately, some pookas on Feyrist are tainted by a mysterious, sinister force that is threatening Feyrist as well as the rest of Tibia.
Swan maidens are fae who can shapeshift from human form to swan form. The magical item allowing this transformation is a swan feather cloak, a garment with swan feathers attached. ...
Here on Feyrist it is always hard to tell whether a swan swimming on a lake is an ordinary animal or a swan maiden in her bird shape. ...
They protect the wilds of their secret realm from every intruder and live in small flocks along secluded lakeshores. As Maelyrra told me, swan maidens emerge from mortals' dreams about flying.

Nymphs are female nature spirits and usually take the form of beautiful, young maidens who love to dance and sing. They dwell in the hills and forests of Feyrist, often near lakes and streams and they can't die of old age nor illness. ...
They have a special, strong bond to the plants and animals of their domain and are very protective of Feyrist's flora and fauna. As Maelyrra told me, nymphs emerge from mortals' dreams about love.
Boogies are a rather twisted kind of fae. Other than pixies, nymphs or fauns they favour underground caves and tunnels over forests or lush meadows. ...

Only at night, they are roaming the surface, chasing other fae and visitors to Feyrist alike. They were once clumsy yet peaceful fae, but they are now twisted and tainted by a mysterious, sinister force.
She's the queen of a fae court. You can find her on a glade in the deep forest. It was queen Maelyrra who granted me permission to stay here in Feyrist. ...
I promised to inform her about anything I find out about the abominable force that threatens this world.

The fae vary greatly in size and appearance. There are different kinds of fae like fauns, pixies, pookas, swan maidens, nymphs and boogies. Those mystical creatures are wielding power in magic and elementals. ...

Most of them are rather reclusive and live peaceful lives in their secret realm. Sometimes they are called the ,children of dreams' or ,the dream born' because the fae are born from the mortals' dreams.
It doesn't seem that the cults share a common theme or object of reverence but there has to be some connection beyond being at the centre of culminations of disruptive power from beyond. ...
The connection is of second thought though. Connected or not, they further the death of our world. That alone makes it imperative to dig those cults out and destroy their cores. ...
We won't be able to rout our each and any movement but if we manage to neutralize the worst, we gain some time and deny the enemy much of its leverage on the future of our world.
We have located some of the worst culminations of otherworldly presence and our sources returned information about them with different results of success. ...
Some information I can provide you will be rather sparse and much is left to speculation but you should have at least some lead where to go and investigate.
Those cults have to be stopped by any means possible. These are desperate times and they demand desperate actions.
Spare lives where you see it fit but the cults may not be allowed to exist and disrupt the fabric of the world even more.
The weakened fabric of our reality still repels the unnatural intruder. The cults provide the thing a hold and supply it with more power, even if we couldn't figure out yet, how this works at all.
We haven't completely figured out what our enemy exactly is. For one, this thing defies all laws of nature and comprehension, ...

that understanding it is either impossible or twist a mind in ways that are not meant to be. Also the Yalahari who figured out way too much about the thing, became tainted and changed by this knowledge ...
And ultimately not only fell and became his, they also provided the thing with something of their own, be it knowledge, understanding or even direction, purpose. ...
In some way their tainted knowledge brought the unthinkable into a resemblance of existence. ...
That is why we cant dabble too much in figuring this out and rather concentrate on our fight to severe its ties to our world.
I have several missions available that deal with servering the ties of the corrupting influence on our world.

greeting: Greetings, traveller |PLAYERNAME|. Where do you want me to {fly} you? Or do you need a weekly ticket for the Kazordoon public lorry transport?

responses: I can fly you to {Darashia}, {Svargrond}, {Edron}, {Issavi}, {Zao}, {Femor Hills} or to {Marapur} if you like. Where do you want to go?
Of course, just browse through my wares.
We are the {guardians}. We are all what is left from the glory of the past.
We are the keepers of this old temple and the guardians of its sacred {secrets}.
We are keepers of the {lore} of the Great {Snake} and we guard some of its {relics}.
The lore of the Great {Snake} is contorted like a snake. Although we feel you are touched by the snake's essence, you are not ready yet to receive the blessing of the deeper wisdom.
Once the powerful relics of the Great {Snake} were abundant. Most got lost to the ravages of time and our enemies. What is {left} is all the more precious.
Some of the relics are still left in this world. They are insignia of the snake god's power. We know that you came for one of those insignia. ...
The Great {Snake} approves that you acquire its holy {sceptre}. We will not question the wisdom of the god to hand this relic to one of the false-born.
We are the guardians. We are all what is left from the glory of the past.

What you call Banuta was once a centre of our civilisation and culture. We gave those {apes} so much, but they wanted more. In the end, the Great {Snake} used them as a tool to teach our people a painful but necessary lesson.

In their vanity our descendants thought they could use those false-born as servants. They paid a terrible price. Their downfall might only be temporary though if we can {redeem} our race in the eyes of the snake god.
We have failed our god and paid a high price. But all tragedies and obstacles are just a test of the Great Snake. We will have to prove we are worthy to be its first true-born. ...
Only in the face of seemingly impossible odds, we can prove what we are capable of. If we overcome all obstacles, the favour of the Great {Snake} will return to us. ...
The god has cast us down only to elevate us to new heights if we are worthy.
Dragons are one of the greatest creations of the Great {Snake}. Yet, they are still distanced from the Great {Snake} and susceptible for {corruption} and the lies of the egg stealers.

There are many forms of corruption in this world. All of it is the work of the egg stealers who spread their taint. The creation hatched by the Great {Snake} was perfect without even the option of corruption.
We understand what he is and what he has become. We were not aware there were others like us left in the world. ...
Perhaps there is still a chance our race can redeem itself in the eyes of the Great {Snake}.
The father and mother of everything. Its first egg hatched the world. Its second egg hatched life itself. The lizard people were of its third litter. ...
Of the third litter but the first true-born, we became its keepers. As its first true children, we inherited the earth but it was taken from us by the {egg stealers}.
Creatures from beyond. Greedy and traitorous, they stole parts of the world and of life for themselves. They corrupted the world by their deceptions and stole our {birthright}.
We were the rulers of the world. We overlooked the world in the name of the Great {Snake}. The egg stealers and their creatures stole it from our claws. ...
We have failed the Great {Snake} and our {decline} is its punishment.
We were overthrown by those of who we vainly thought we could use for our advantage, even though we knew about the foulness of the false-born.
Everything that lacks scales is no true litter of the Great Snake. They are the corrupted kin of the egg stealers.
Such concepts are of little consequence for the dead.
They are a manifestation of the snake god's power. They are its avatars in some way.
We are the true-born. We are the children of the snake. But as children we have yet much to learn. One day we might take our rightful place in creation, but only if we prove {worthy}.
The Great Snake deserves that we try to use the best of our abilities and capabilities to serve it. Our existence is a test in this world corrupted by the egg stealers. ...
Only when we overcome all obstacles cast into our way, we will become worthy to serve the Great Snake in all eternity.
They are the false-born. They hatched from foul eggs - poisoned by the egg stealers. They are numerous and like a plague for the world.
This was once the centre of power of our religion. From here we ruled Tiquanda in the name of the snake god.
We were not aware of another civilisation that was left. Only recently the awareness of the Great Snake touched us in our slumber and we learnt through visions of its other children.
Some of the relics are still left in this world. They are insignia of the snake god's power. We know that you came for one of those insignia. ...
The Great Snake approves that you acquire its holy sceptre. We will not question the wisdom of the god to hand this relic to one of the false-born.
We were once the heigh priests of the Great Snake. We willingly left our mortal existence behind to become bound to this temple. ...
In this state we can serve the Great Snake in a more suitable way. You have to understand that it is an honour and a privilege, not a sacrifice.
Of course, just browse through my wares.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Hi there! Welcome to the spike.
Hi there! I'm ready to brief you with {tactical} advice.
Greetings, member of the Bigfoot Brigade. We could really use some {help} from you right now. You should prove {worthy} to our alliance.

responses: If you're willing to help us, we could need an escort for arriving {ordnance}, help with {charting} the cave system and someone needs to get some heat {measurements} fast.

You're already known amongst the gnomes, member of the Bigfoot Brigade. I will make sure that the alliance learns of your deeds but you'll still need to help the dwarves and gnomes of this outpost to show your worth. ...
We also found {suspicious devices} carried by all kinds of creatures down here. Down here, they are of extreme worth to us since they could contain the key to what's happening all around us. ...
If you can aquire any, return them to me and I make sure to tell the others of your generosity. Return to me afterwards to check on your current {status}.
Gnomish supplies and ingenuity have helped to establish and fortify this outpost. ...
Our knowledge of the enemy and it's tactics would be of more use if the dwarves would listen to us somewhat more. But gnomes have learned to live with the imperfection of the other races.
Our surveys of the area showed us some spikes in heat and seismic activity at very specific places. ...
We conclude this is no coincidence and the enemy is using devices to pump up the lava to flood the area. We have seen it before and had to retreat each time. ...
This time though we might have a counter prepared - given me manage to pierce their defences.
My name is Gnomus.
I'm the main gnomish contact for this base. I coordinate our efforts with those of the dwarves to ensure everything is running smoothly.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

Ah, my whole proud: My ship named SeaHawk. We rode out so many stormy nights together. I think I couldn't live without it.
Ah, my whole proud: My ship named SeaHawk. We rode out so many stormy nights together. I think I couldn't live without it.
I'm a merchant. I sail all over the world with my ship and trade with many different races!
A merchant is someone who trades goods with other people and tries to make a little profit. *laughs*
I trade nearly everything, for example weapons, food, water, and even magic runes.
You know; elves, dwarfs, lizardmen, minotaurs and many others.
Sorry, sold out.
Pssst. Marlene is not near right now...? You know... she is a lovely woman, but she talks too much! So I always try to keep distance from her because she can't stop talking.
Bruno is one of the best sailors I know. He is nearly as good as me. *laughs loudly*

A great name, isn't it?
Beautiful Tibia. And with our help everyone is save.
It is time to join the Knights!
Knights are the warriors of Tibia. Without us, no one would be safe. Every brave and strong man or woman can join us.
Some day someone will make something happen to him...
A bow might be a fine weapon for someone not strong enough to wield a REAL weapon.
I and my students often share a cask of beer or wine at Frodo's hut.
Always concerned with his profit. What a loss! He was adventuring with baxter in the old days.

Before she became a priest she won the Miss Tibia contest three times in a row.
Peaceful farmers.
A fine game to hunt. But be careful, he cheats!
Bah, go away with these sorcerer tricks. Only cowards use tricks.
What an idiot.
I will never understand this peaceful monks and priests.
He has the muscles, but lacks the guts.

The bank is on the left side of the depot. Eva will be at your service there.
You can buy weapons, armor, tools, gems, magical equipment, furniture, spells and food here.
The depot is a place where you can safely store your belongings. You are also protected against attacks there. I escort newcomers there.
I'll help you find your way through Carlin, the amazon town. I can mark important locations on your map and give you some information about the town and the world status.
This city is ruled by Queen Eloise with the help of her female brigade. We have a lot of shops here as well as the usual facilities like depot, temple and so on.
I'm Alexena, at your service!

This city has three main districts - a noble district, a merchant district and the slums. The class difference is clearly visible here. Most shops are in the merchant district. There are also large sugarcane plantations.
I'm Davina. Glad to help you today.

You can buy weapons, armor, tools, gems, magical equipment, furniture, spells and food here.

Yalahar is a very old city of gigantic size, consisting of a city centre and eight quarters. The beings that are closest to be called rulers of the city are the Yalahari.

greeting: Welcome, |PLAYERNAME|! Lovely steamboat, ain't it? I can even offer you a {passage} if you like.

responses: Where do you want me to take you? To {Kazordoon}, {Farmine} or {Gnomprona}?
Of course, just browse through my wares.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Welcome |PLAYERNAME|! See the fine {weapons} I sell.

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.
I sell the finest weapons in town. If you'd like to see my offers, ask me for a {trade}.
Of course, just browse through my wares.
Me great {wizard}. Me great doctor of {ape people}. Me know many plants. Me old and me have seen many things.
We see many things and learning quick. Merlkin magic learn quick, quick. We just watch and learn. Sometimes we try and learn.
Things not good now. Need helper to do {mission} for me people.
We be {kongra}, {sibang} and {merlkin}. Strange hairless ape people live in city called Port Hope.
Kongra verry strong. Kongra verry angry verry fast. Take care when kongra comes. Better climb on highest tree.
Sibang verry fast and funny. Sibang good gather food. Sibang know {jungle} well.
Merlkin we are. Merlkin verry wise, merlkin learn many things quick. Teach other apes things a lot. Making {heal} and making {magic}.
We see many things and learning quick. Merlkin magic learn quick, quick. We just watch and learn. Sometimes we try and learn.
Jungle is dangerous. Jungle also provides us food. Take care when in jungle and safe you be.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Hello competitor! Do you want to {fight} in the arena or shall I explain the {rules} first?

responses: What do you want to know? Something about the three different {difficulties}, the {general} rules or the {prices}? Maybe you also want to know what happens when you {die}?
There are three difficulties: Greenhorn, Scrapper and Warlord. On each challenge you will be confronted with ten monsters increasing in strength.
There are three difficulties: Greenhorn, Scrapper and Warlord. On each challenge you will be confronted with ten monsters increasing in strength.
There are three difficulties: Greenhorn, Scrapper and Warlord. On each challenge you will be confronted with ten monsters increasing in strength.
That is the easiest way in our arena. The {fee} is 1000 gold. We were setting this up for of our children to challenge some easy monsters and train them for the future.
The most common difficulty for us. The {fee} is 5000 gold. So if you are experienced in fighting middle class monsters this is your challenge!
Only the strongest among us will take this challenge. The {fee} is 10000 gold. If you pass that I promise you the respect of all citizens here. You will be a hero!
The fee is either 1000, 5000 or 10000 gold for one try. Remember that if you {die}, it is YOUR problem and you won't be able to get back to your corpse and your backpack.

It would be better not to die! In every pit there is an emergency exit, the portal to the south. If you die in a pit... well... your corpse and backpack are gone, so you enter the arena at your own risk.
Basically you pay me a {fee}, and you are sent into an arena with 10 different stages. If you succeed you will be rewarded accordingly.
My job is to explain about the rules and to get the fee from the competitors.
Well I would rather call it an {Ultimate Challenge} than a mission.

greeting: Hi there, fellow adventurer. What's your need? Say {trade} and we'll soon get you fixed up. Or ask me about {potions}, {wands}, or {runes}.

responses: Take your pick! Or maybe you want to look only at {potions}, {wands} or {runes}?
Hamish MacGuffin, at your disposal.
I craft highly efficient runes, wands and potions - always handy when you're in a fight against monsters! Ask me for a trade to browse through my stock.
Nope. Doesn't sound familiar.
Never asked about his past. Seems it's a pretty gloomy one. But he's an excellent blacksmith and seems content enough with his work here.

Inigo taught me a trick or two since I joined Mr Morris' little crowd. Quite a nice old chap who's seen much of the world and knows his way around here. You should definitely ask him if you need help.
Can be quite feisty if you doubt his seniorship. <snorts> Knows a thing or two about spells, though. Useful knowledge.
Keeran? He's a bit like Plunderpurse's shadow, isn't he? Loves numbers, but I believe underneath it all he always wanted to break out of his boring little job in the city.
Seems good-natured enough a guy. Nimble with his hands, be it cooking or carpentering. Seems to want to keep his mind off something, so he's always busy fixing stuff or cooking something up.

If it wasn't for Mr Morris, maybe none of us would be alive. Or at least, none of us would be here. He's been everywhere and gathered some adventurers around him to investigate the secrets of Dawnport.
Our druid, down in the temple. Just appeared out of the blue one day. Keeps to herself. She must have some reason to stay with us rather than roam the bigger Mainland. Well, we all have our reasons.
Now there's someone who has lived life to the full! Don't know though whether I should really believe that he's a clerk now.

I wish I had thought of changing my name to that of a hero. Would have smoothed my way no end! Anyway, whatever he was before he joined, Tybald now fits the bill of the legendary hero. He even has a crush on lady Oressa. Cute. <chuckles>
Hah, I told him he should take a potion or two! But nooo, he's always so sure of himself. Huh. Well, you'd better tell {Oressa} to patch him up, then.
Of course, just browse through my wares. You can also look at {wands} or {runes}.
Of course, just browse through my wares. You can also look at {potions} or {runes}.
Of course, just browse through my wares. You can also look at {potions} or {wands}.

Small and deceptively friendly-looking island. Well, I used to study the plants and herbs here for my potions. Nowadays, I leave that to Oressa, she has a better way with that horrible wildlife here. I prefer to distil potions in the quiet of my lab. If you need some potions, runes or other magic equipment, ask for a trade.

Dawnport is not far off from the coast of the Tibian Mainland. Lots of cities, monsters, bandits, brigands, mean folk and people of low understanding with no sense of respect towards alchemical genius. <mutters to himself> Ahem. Once you're level 8, you should be experienced enough to choose your definite vocation and leave Dawnport for Main - and Tibia definitely needs more skilled adventurers to keep those monsters in check which roam our lands!
I've seen Mr Morris leaving the key here and there on several occasions. He really should have put it somewhere safe.

greeting: Oh, please come in, |PLAYERNAME|. What do you need? Have a look at my wonderful {offers} in gems and jewellery.

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.
I am a jeweler. Maybe you want to have a look at my wonderful offers.
I am Hanna.

greeting: Hello |PLAYERNAME|! Do you need my services?
farewell: Good bye. Visit me whenever you want to sell something.
walkAway: Good bye. Visit me whenever you want to sell something.

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.
I am buying some weapons and armors.

greeting: What do you want, |PLAYERNAME|? I'm a {busy} man.

responses: I have a {job}, you know?
Well, I'm kind of a delivery man I guess. I take on small {transportation} jobs with my boat.

greeting: Want to go back to {Yalahar} for 50 gold? Just ask me.

farewell: It was a pleasure to help you, |PLAYERNAME|.

responses: Keep in mind you won't find better offers here. Just browse through my wares.

farewell: Farewell! May the serene light of the enlightened one rest shine on your travels.

responses: At your service, just browse through my wares.

farewell: LONG LIVE THE KING! You may leave now!

For level 10 I have {Cure Poison} for 150 gold.
For level 9 I have {Magic Rope} for 200 gold.
For level 8 I have {Find Person} for 80 gold, {Light} for free and {Light Healing} for free.
For level 1 I have {Arrow Call} for free and {Magic Patch} for free.

walkAway: This ungraceful haste is most suspicious!

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.
It's a shame that only a few paladins still use their abilities to further the cause of the gods of good. Too many paladins have become selfish and greedy.
Nowadays, most knights seem to have forgotten the noble cause to which all knights were bound in the past. Only a few have remained pious, serve the gods and follow their teachings.
Those who wield great power have to resist great temptations. We have the burden to eliminate all those who give in to the temptations.
The druids here still follow the old rules. Sadly, the druids of Carlin have left the right path in the last years.
The dwarfs are allied with Thais but follow their own obscure religion. Although dwarfs keep mostly to themselves, we have to observe this alliance closely.
The dwarfs are allied with Thais but follow their own obscure religion. Although dwarfs keep mostly to themselves, we have to observe this alliance closely.
Those elves are hardly any more civilised than orcs. They can become a threat to mankind at any time.
Those elves are hardly any more civilised than orcs. They can become a threat to mankind at any time.

Venore is somewhat difficult to handle. The merchants have a close eye on our activities in their city and our authority is limited there. However, we will use all of our influence to prevent a second Carlin.
Drefia used to be a city of sin and heresy, just like Carlin nowadays. One day, the gods decided to destroy this town and to erase all evil there.
Darashia is a godless town full of mislead fools. One day, it will surely share the fate of its sister town Drefia.
Demons exist in many different shapes and levels of power. In general, they are servants of the dark gods and command great powers of destruction.
Carlin is a city of sin and heresy. After the reunion of Carlin with the kingdom, the inquisition will have much work to purify the city and its inhabitants.
We can see his evil influence almost everywhere. Keep your eyes open or the dark one will lead you on the wrong way and destroy you.
The church of Crunor works closely together with the druid guild. This makes a cooperation sometimes difficult.
We owe to the gods of good our creation and continuing existence. If it weren't for them, we would surely fall prey to the minions of the vile and dark gods.
The churches of the gods united to fight heresy and dark magic. They are the shield of the true believers, while the inquisition is the sword that fights all enemies of virtuousness.
The churches of the gods entrusted me with the enormous and responsible task to lead the inquisition. I leave the field work to inquisitors who I recruit from fitting people that cross my way.
Belive on the gods and they will show you the path.
By edict of the churches I'm the Lord Inquisitor.
I'm Henricus, the Lord Inquisitor.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Be greeted, |PLAYERNAME|. The {spiritual} world looks upon you and your deeds.

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.
Would you like to receive that protection for a sacrifice of |BLESSCOST| gold, child?
Fine. You are free to decline my offer.
You aren't looking that bad. Sorry, I can't help you. But if you are looking for additional protection you should go on the {pilgrimage} of ashes or get the protection of the {twist of fate} here.

Whenever you receive a lethal wound, your vital force is damaged and there is a chance that you lose some of your equipment. With every single of the five {blessings} you have, this damage and chance of loss will be reduced.

There are five blessings available in five sacred places: the {spiritual} shielding, the spark of the {phoenix}, the {embrace} of Tibia, the fire of the {suns} and the wisdom of {solitude}. Additionally, you can receive the {twist of fate} here.
You can ask for the blessing of spiritual shielding in the whiteflower temple south of Thais.
You can ask for the blessing of the two suns in the suntower near Ab'Dendriel.
The spark of the phoenix is given by the dwarven priests of earth and fire in Kazordoon.
Talk to the hermit Eremo on the isle of Cormaya about this blessing.
I'm just a simple {huntsman}.
Sorry, I don't think telling a stranger your name is a smart thing to do.
Here. Don't forget, if you buy potions, there's a {deposit} of 5 gold on the empty flask.
Time doesn't matter to me.
I'm Hyacinth.
I'm a {druid} and healer, a follower of {Crunor}.
May Crunor bless you and protect you on your journeys!
There are only two other druids on Rookgaard, {Lily} and {Cipfried}.
Storage for worldly wealth.
Who knows what it will be? Only time will show.
Thank the gods, I'm fine.
What help do you seek? I sell health potions, ask me for a {trade} if you need one.
I can't teach you magic. On the {mainland} you will learn spells early enough.
I'm one of the few magic users on this isle. I can sense a follower of the dark path of magic hiding somewhere in the depths of the dungeons.
It is shaped by the will of the gods, so we don't have to question it.
I used to be there with my old friend Cipfried to heal adventurers. After all these years, I prefer solitude now.
Teaching you about the gods would require too much time. But you can always read the books in the {library}.
The library is in the {academy}, north of the {temple}.
A place to learn about {Tibia}.
Are you hungry? I'm sorry, I have no food here.
Yes, I sell small health potions. Ask me for a trade if you need one.
I don't care about kings, queens, and the like.
I rarely visit the town. It's much better here.
There's a huge world waiting for you.
Most of the so-called monsters of this isle are only creatures of the gods. There are some beasts that are truly monstrous on the {mainland}.
Are you hungry? I'm sorry, I have no food here.
The dungeons are dangerous for inexperienced adventurers.
Just ask me for a {trade} to check out my offers. I have only small health potions for sale, though.
You'll have to buy that from the merchants in town. I'm just a simple druid and healer.
I'll pay you 5 gold for every empty vial and potion flask. Just ask me for a {trade}.
A greedy and annoying person just like most people are.
Now she has completely gotten out of her mind.
He was a promising young druid when something happened to his mind. It's a sad story.
He does a good job out there.
I know her since she was little.
One of these greedy merchants.
I saw her stranding with her raft.
He's a farmer and behaves a little better than his cousin.
An unpleasant person.
His healing powers equal mine.
I think she's under bad influence.
That's my name.
She's actually quite nice.
She's a druid. Since she started selling health potions, people visit me only rarely. Which is a good thing, but of course I'll help if I'm needed.
The oracle will lead you to your {destiny} once you are level 8.
I think that guy is new. He's a {bank} clerk.
He has some inner devils that torture him.
Tom is the local tanner. That means he always needs fresh corpses or leather.
A man of the sword.

greeting: Hello |PLAYERNAME|! What do you need? If it's {food}, you've come to the right place.

responses: Yep, take a good look.
I offer quite a lot of food. Ask me for a {trade} if you're hungry
I can bring you either to the {east} end of Port Hope or to the {centre} of the town, where would you like to go?

greeting: You came through the {portal}! Though it must be different from where you came from, I'm sure you can help us. But first, I can {help} YOU.

responses: Ah, back in the old days, I was called Inigo Verasquiriz, but I doubt my family would acknowledge me. Those that are still alive, that is.
I used to be a hunter and trapper, but these bones have grown weary. I now tan furs and hides and {trade} in creature products. And I can help you find your way if you need directions.
That's where we are - the world of Tibia. We're on a small island called {Dawnport}, not far from the coast of the Tibian {Mainland}, to be precise.
Oh, I can handle my tasks myself, thank you. If you are looking for something to do, you should go to Morris and ask him for quests. You can also help us fight {monsters}.
Well, the other fellows you see here, selling stuff and so forth. Say a name and I'll tell you what I know of him or her.
Marvellous specimen we have here on {Dawnport}! Just be careful when you go hunting them, {dying} hurts in {Tibia}! I will happily buy any creature products you may find.
Used to be a professor at the magic academy in Edron, I believe. Likes to see different places, so he joined our illustrous little group. Sells {sorcerer} and {druid} {spells} to our trainees.
I'm not sure that's his actual name, but I guess he has good reasons to stay incognito here. In any case, he's a formidable fighter and knows usefull {spells} for knights and paladins.

Richard came here as a shipwrecked carpenter, and now sells {tools} and {food} to all adventurers. If you need a {rope} or {shovel}, {fishing rod} or some provisions for a hunt, you should trade with him!
Hasn't been with us for very long. Very interested in all things mystical.
Knights are close combat fighters, the toughest vocation of all. They usually wield melee weapons such as swords, axes or clubs.
Druids are nature magicians. Their speciality is casting ice and earth magic, as well as providing healing for others.
Paladins are well-trained distance fighters and can cast holy magic. You will usually see them wearing bows or crossbows.
Sorcerers are elemental magicians. They have mastered fire, energy and death magic.

In {Tibia}, you can use different tools with your environment, or for quests. The most important tools are {rope} and {shovel}. A {fishing rod}'s also good when you need to eat, and are near a river or the sea.

Food is very important for your health and mana. If you are hurt in a fight with a monster, select 'USE' on food such as cheese, ham or meat to eat it. This will slowly refill your health and mana. Here at the outpost, you can buy food from {Richard}.

Use a fishing rod on a patch of water to see if you can catch fish! Eating is essential in {Tibia} - if you don't eat when you're hungry, you won't regenerate health or mana. So you should aways have some {food} with you.

I've seen that goblin Woblin sneaking around the place, and more than once he was eyeing the key suspiciously. ... Woblin sometimes visits the outpost for a trade. Something of a loner, not like your usual goblin. ... He lives in a cave somewhere to the west of the isle. On a map, that would be on the left-hand side.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Welcome, young |PLAYERNAME|! If you are heavily wounded or poisoned, I can {heal} you for free or you want the {adventurer stone}?
walkAway: Remember: If you are heavily wounded or poisoned, I can heal you for free.

responses: You already received my line of the prayer.
This is a special blessing I can bestow upon you once you have obtained at least one of the other blessings and which functions a bit differently. ...
It only works when you're killed by other adventurers, which means that at least half of the damage leading to your death was caused by others, not by monsters or the environment. ...

The {twist of fate} will not reduce the death penalty like the other blessings, but instead prevent you from losing your other blessings as well as the amulet of loss, should you wear one. It costs the same as the other blessings. ...
Would you like to receive that protection for a sacrifice of |PVPBLESSCOST| gold, child?
Fine. You are free to decline my offer.
Ah, you want to replace your adventurer's stone for 30 gold?
Sorry, you don't have enough money.
No problem.
You aren't looking that bad. Sorry, I can't help you. But if you are looking for additional protection you should go on the {pilgrimage} of ashes or get the protection of the {twist of fate} here.

Whenever you receive a lethal wound, your vital force is damaged and there is a chance that you lose some of your equipment. With every single of the five {blessings} you have, this damage and chance of loss will be reduced.

There are five blessings available in five sacred places: the {spiritual} shielding, the spark of the {phoenix}, the {embrace} of Tibia, the fire of the {suns} and the wisdom of {solitude}. Additionally, you can receive the {twist of fate} here.
You can ask for the blessing of spiritual shielding in the whiteflower temple south of Thais.
The druids north of Carlin will provide you with the embrace of Tibia.
You can ask for the blessing of the two suns in the suntower near Ab'Dendriel.
The spark of the phoenix is given by the dwarven priests of earth and fire in Kazordoon.
Talk to the hermit Eremo on the isle of Cormaya about this blessing.
Of course, just browse through my wares.
Would you like to learn {conjure explosive arrow} magic spell for 1000 gold?
Would you like to learn {conjure arrow} magic spell for 450 gold?
Would you like to learn {divine healing} magic spell for 3000 gold?
Would you like to learn {lesser ethereal spear} magic spell for free?
Would you like to learn {arrow call} magic spell for free?
I can teach you {attack} spells, {healing} spells and {support} spells. What kind of spell do you wish to learn? I can also tell you which spells are available at your {level}.
My attack spell is: {Lesser Ethereal Spear}.
My healing spells are: {Divine Healing}.
My support spells are: {Arrow Call}, {Conjure Arrow} and {Conjure Explosive Arrow}.
I have spells for level {1}, {13}, {25} and {35}.
For level 35 I have {Divine Healing} for 3000 gold.
For level 25 I have {Conjure Explosive Arrow} for 1000 gold.
For level 13 I have {Conjure Arrow} for 450 gold.
For level 1 I have {Arrow Call} for free and {Lesser Ethereal Spear} for free.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Welcome to the house of fashion, |PLAYERNAME|!

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Be mourned, pilgrim in flesh. Are you looking for a promotion?

responses: Do you want to be promoted in your vocation for 20000 gold?
Ok, whatever.

greeting: Be greeted, |PLAYERNAME|. Which of my fine gems do you {need}?

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.
I am a jeweller. Maybe you want to have a look at my wonderful {offers}.
Well, I sell gems and {goblets}. If you'd like to see my offers, ask me for a {trade}.
Ah, our newest import! We have golden goblets, silver goblets and bronze goblets. All of them have space for a hand-written dedication.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Welcome, young |PLAYERNAME|! If you are heavily wounded or poisoned, I can {heal} you for free.
walkAway: Remember: If you are heavily wounded or poisoned, I can heal you for free.

responses: This is a special blessing I can bestow upon you once you have obtained at least one of the other blessings and which functions a bit differently. ...
It only works when you're killed by other adventurers, which means that at least half of the damage leading to your death was caused by others, not by monsters or the environment. ...

The {twist of fate} will not reduce the death penalty like the other blessings, but instead prevent you from losing your other blessings as well as the amulet of loss, should you wear one. It costs the same as the other blessings. ...
Would you like to receive that protection for a sacrifice of |PVPBLESSCOST| gold, child?
Fine. You are free to decline my offer.
Ah, you want to replace your adventurer's stone for 30 gold?
Sorry, you don't have enough money.
No problem.
A blessed stake? That's a strange request. Maybe Quentin knows more, he is one of the oldest monks after all.
You aren't looking that bad. Sorry, I can't help you. But if you are looking for additional protection you should go on the {pilgrimage} of ashes or get the protection of the {twist of fate} here.

Whenever you receive a lethal wound, your vital force is damaged and there is a chance that you lose some of your equipment. With every single of the five {blessings} you have, this damage and chance of loss will be reduced.

There are five blessings available in five sacred places: the {spiritual} shielding, the spark of the {phoenix}, the {embrace} of Tibia, the fire of the {suns} and the wisdom of {solitude}. Additionally, you can receive the {twist of fate} here.
You can ask for the blessing of spiritual shielding in the whiteflower temple south of Thais.
The druids north of Carlin will provide you with the embrace of Tibia.
You can ask for the blessing of the two suns in the suntower near Ab'Dendriel.
The spark of the phoenix is given by the dwarven priests of earth and fire in Kazordoon.
Talk to the hermit Eremo on the isle of Cormaya about this blessing.
I run the dog sled service from this city to {Nibelor}.
I am Iskan.
I can bring you to Nibelor with the sled if you want to. Just ask for a {passage}.
This city seems to be growing every day. First, there have been only a few huts and now ... look around, houses everywhere.
Our people are very proud and honest. I guess that's more than most people can say about themselves and their people, right boys?
I heard something about a ship crashing in the shelf ice between Nibelor and Grimlund. If I were you, I wouldn't go to chakoya terrain. It's pretty dangerous.
Nasty little beasts. Me and the boys had quite some bloody encounters with them on the ice.
The boys warn me in advance if a monster is around.
My boys did not like the guys that were travelling through our city, and I trust in their instincts. Isn't it so, boys?
The yeti is real and dangerous.
I am not sure what to think about this new custom to accept foreigners as one of us after passing that test. But it's the Jarl's decision, not mine.
The raiders camps are far to the south. There are three of them. Small camp sites of raiding parties might be found almost anywhere though.
They are sometimes trying to catch me and the boys. Such fools, they will never get us. Right, boys?
The boys can tell who's an enemy just by the smell.
I'll tell you something. I have seen such a creature once, far on the ice to the east. It was the coldest winter I can remember and the chakoyas were roaming the ice almost everywhere. ...
To evade them, me and the boys had to walk a long way to a spot where the ice was treacherous and thin. ...
And there I've seen such a creature. The boys went almost mad and I turned my sled immediately. No idea how we made it home. True story.
Me and the boys care only about our own territory.
I guess their healing powers are almost as useful as those of our shamans.
I guess there are guys like me that have a talent to talk to dogs and there are guys like the shamans that have the talent to talk to spirits.
We don't need any kings or queens. Am I right, boys? Yeah, I am.
Sven the Younger is our current Jarl. For my standards he's still a puppy, but a smart one at least.
Yeah, where could it be. That's the question.
Easy, boys! Don't mention that name again. Makes the boys nuts, ye know?
In my whole long life, I've not seen any sign of those gods the people of the south are constantly talking about.
The icy father. We know him all too well, am I right boys? He has claimed so many old friends in all those years. So many.

greeting: Hello, worshiper of the Astral Shapers! Welcome to the temple! While you are here to pay tribute, you can also learn something about the ancient art of imbuing.

responses: My {father}, can you tell me if he's alright?
Alright then, you are very welcome to explore the temple!
My name is Ivalisse.
There is always time to make a change.
Besides my various {duties} in the temple, I also take care of visitors. Well, I would but right now I can't get my mind of how my {father}'s doing. I am sorry.
I help linking the portals of this temple to other ancient sites of the {Astral Shapers}.
I help linking the portals of this temple to other ancient sites of the {Astral Shapers}.

greeting: Feel welcome to my gem store, |PLAYERNAME|.

responses: Here, take a look.

greeting: Hiho Storm Killer! Welcome to Kazordoon furniture store.

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Finally! A c...c...customer! Wh... where do you want to f...f...{fly}?
farewell: D...Daraman's blessings... oh, how I m...miss my warm Darashia...

responses: I'm a licensed c...carpet pilot. I can {transport} you t...to a warm p...place.
I t...{transport} travellers to the continent of Darama for a small fee. It's w...warm there.
I can fly you to {Darashia}, to {Edron}, to {Kazordoon}, to Z... Zao, to the F...{Femor Hills}, to {Issavi} or to M... {Marapur} if you like. W....Where do you want to go?
I can fly you to {Darashia}, to {Edron}, to {Kazordoon}, to Z... Zao, to the F...{Femor Hills}, to {Issavi} or to M... {Marapur} if you like. W....Where do you want to go?
I can fly you to {Darashia}, to {Edron}, to {Kazordoon}, to Z... Zao, to the F...{Femor Hills}, to {Issavi} or to M... {Marapur} if you like. W....Where do you want to go?
I'm k...known as Iyad Ibn Abdul.
The thought of s...some mad sorcerer unleashing some f...fire magic is very tempting.
S...so close and s...still that far away.
For all its m...mistakes, Thais is warm at least.
I can fly you to {Darashia}, to {Edron}, to {Kazordoon}, to Z... Zao, to the F...{Femor Hills}, to {Issavi} or to M... {Marapur} if you like. W....Where do you want to go?
I would t...trade it for some firewood.
This h...here is no continent. This is h...hell.
Even D...Drefia can't be worse than this land.
I d...don't care about any n...news unless it's the announcement of a h....heatwave.
It's p...probably the ice age.
Th...there, in case you have {earmuffs}, I pay w...well!
Do you really have earmuffs f...for me?? If so, just ask me for a {trade}!

farewell: Good bye. Recommend us if you were satisfied with our service.

responses: Where do you want to go? To {Edron}, {Thais}, {Venore}, {Darashia}, {Ankrahmun}, {Yalahar} or {Port Hope}?
Where do you want to go? To {Edron}, {Thais}, {Venore}, {Darashia}, {Ankrahmun}, {Yalahar} or {Port Hope}?
My name is Jack Fate from the Royal Tibia Line.
I'm the captain of this sailing ship.
I'm the captain of this sailing ship.
That's where we are.

greeting: Hello, Sir |PLAYERNAME|. Where can I {sail} you today?

responses: My name is Jack Fate from the Royal Tibia Line.
I'm the captain of this - well, wreck. Argh.
I'm the captain of this - well, wreck. Argh
This is where we are... the volcano island Goroma. There are many rumours about this place.
Of course, my friend.
I need your help in a matter of utmost {urgency}.
The situation is complicated and it's even hard to say where to {start} best, just to describe it to you.
You see, several incidents in history can be traced back to a single {source}.
Things get murkier the further you go down in history, but that's not even necessary. Even today we can discern a certain pattern in recent {events}.
Well, in Rathleton there was an individual at work, looking for some ancient artefact of power. ...
To cover its escape the creature left another creature, known as the ravager to cover his tracks. But there is {more}.
Only recently someone was trying to manipulate the elven dream courts into releasing a monstrosity of nightmares, probably planning to control or recruit this creature. ....
But those incidents were just some of {many}.
The recent rise of lycanthropy, the robbery of certain forbidden arcane texts and the vanishing of at least three dangerous individuals, targeted by the inquisition are just the tip of the {iceberg}.
There is a scheming going on behind the scenes. Powerful good people were corrupted. Evil-doers got backup and resources from a hidden ally. ...
Powerful malignant creatures, gathering their kind under their banner and so much more. These things are not happening by chance. There is a pattern, a guiding {hand}.
This outside force is moving behind the scenes since ages. Our research suggests that this force probably even {predates} the rise of humanity.

Well, we are sure that the puppeteer behind all these events is an organisation. So old that even its name, the Shiron'Fal, has lost its meaning, because the {language} it originates from is long dead.
It has a rather complex meaning and as far as we can tell it translates to 'army of those who are many, dedicated to the ultimate time of mayhem and despair'. ...
Other, more handy names are army of the last battlefield, army of the last days, legion of mayhem, dread legion or simply the {legion}.
We know little for sure. You can look into our books to see some of our sources. But most are vague and some even contradictory. ...
To summarise what we know, let me tell you this: The Shiron'Fal is an extremely old organisation. It seeks to accumulate power for some unknown but certainly sinister {goal}.

For this purpose, the members gather knowledge, artefacts and powerful individuals. The members are formidable at certain fields of expertise. They are cunning and powerful and act with no regard for others, with no remorse or mercy. ...
As they are doing this since ages, they must have acquired tremendous powers and knowledge. Their members often operate alone but are usually well funded with the necessary resources. ...
Whatever their endgame might be, each of their operations pose a grave danger to the whole world and have to be {stopped}.
Here is where you come into play. We could identify the most recent plot of the Shiron'Fal and already had some {clashes}.
In our efforts to hinder their plot, we achieved mixed results at best. But now things are escalating fast and we have to {hurry}.
Our resources are already stretched thin, so we need your help with the most recent {problem}.
The legion tries to use a new form of twisted rituals to raise the bodies of well-known {knights}.
The knights they aim at were tainted in life by their actions or happenstance. ...
This leaves their bodies vulnerable to their special breed of necromancy that would raise them as powerful {lich}-knights.
These powerful undead were a terrible threat on their own but it seems even they are just part of some larger {scheme} that we cannot make out yet.
We are still working feverishly to uncover their goals but for now more imminent {threats} are at hand.
Death cultists of the Shiron'Fal are trying to locate the bodies of fallen knights and raise them in blasphemous {rituals}.
The churches of the gods worked hand in hand to supply us with the means to {purge} the graves of those knights.
Reaching the graves will not be without danger and if you encounter the death cultists you will have to fight them. Even worse, they might have even succeeded in some cases. ...
As a newly risen lich-knight is not able to leave the site of its resurrection for some time, you might have to fight some of them. ...
Let us pray that you never come too {late} or else some of the fiends might be able to leave their crypts.

We have located twelve graves that have to be secured: In the old northern Edron graveyard, in the dark cathedral of the plains of havoc, in the ghostlands, on Cormaya, Somewhere in the Femor Hills, on Vengoth, ...

in the graveyard of Darashia, in the old temple north of Thais, at the entrance to the orcland, one is on the southern ice islands, in a mountain on Kilmaresh, one on an island north-east of Ankrahmun.

greeting: Hello. Sorry, but I'm not in the best {mood} today.

responses: The only animal to catch there is the cobra and most people don't like to keep one as a pet.
Who wants such an animal as a pet?!?!
Those amazons denied making business with me. You know the reason?!? I tell you, the reason was that I am MALE! Ridiculous!
For sure, I would have offered cats!
He gave me the permission to open up a pet shop here. I paid him almost everything I got!
I made contact with Svargrond! A guy named Buddel wanted to sell me a three-headed dog. I haven't seen it yet, but imagine how much money I could make by breeding such a pet!!
I like it here, at least the town. I was warned that there are nasty creatures outside the town walls but I didn't expect anything to happen so close to town!
I was always good with animals. My parents had a farm, the McRonalds farm. Maybe you know it. Anyway, I had the idea that people may like to have animals as companions and so one led to another.
I'd go mad listening to this 'ribbit' the whole time. But each to his own!
I'm new here in Edron. I wanted to open up a pet shop. But now I'm ruined, uahahahaha <sniffs>.
I need to write a letter to the king. I'm not sure if those 2 million gold coins that Daniel Steelsoul demanded from me weren't too much.
I'm there from time to time, catching frogs. People like their bright colours.
My name is Jerom but no need to remember my name. All my belongings are destroyed and stolen. I'll be gone soon!
Raymond Striker offered me a contract to export parrots from Meriana but I won't need them anymore now that I'm ruined.
Not a very good spot to catch animals. At least no animals that pass off as pets.
Yesterday I would have said that I like all creatures of Tibia but TODAY my life has changed! When I catch this stinking troll!!
True, why not keep a rat as a pet! They can eat your trash, live inside your walls and you don't have to worry if one dies ! Could be a huge success....if I weren't ruined!!!!
That would be a mission for a hero.
Well, I don't see any other way than going back to my home country now.
Good idea! Easy to catch and once the owner has been bitten, he can't sue me anymore, hehehe!!.... but wait....I forgot....I'm ruined, uahahahaha <sniffs>.
I never make profit in Thais and most of the time I get robbed. I stopped visiting Thais for business some years ago.
I don't have anything to earn a living with, everything is destroyed! Otherwise I could offer you pets.
I saw a troll rummaging in the remains of my house last night! I'm sure this creature is behind that! But I am too weak to take revenge.
I've been to town and on my way back I heard a deafening sound. Then I saw that the sound came from a huge rock smashing my house.

It's about time you showed the vampires that they should never bother the citizens again.
Another vampire raid last night. But then again, that's nothing new.
Well, I should be the one to thank you I guess.

greeting: Hmm... surely you are in need of {help} - will you let me? I am {Kais} the Bound and I can lend you a hand in {healing} your body and soul or even grant an {enhanced} blessing!

responses: I have the power to grant you the blood of the mountain's blessing. But I must ask of you to sacrifice |BLESSCOST| gold. Are you prepared for that?
Fine. You are free to decline my offer.
You aren't looking that bad. Sorry, I can't help you. But if you are looking for additional protection you should go on the {pilgrimage} of ashes or get the protection of the {twist of fate} here.
I am Kais, Kais the Bound. Eternally {fixed} to the wretched place, unless... unless I prove my worth in aiding all those who seek my {help}.
Well, as I am quite in a fix currently, my only hope to escape this situation may be to grant you {healing} or an {enhanced} blessing if you wish. You must desire SOMETHING, right? Sure you do.

Besides the {enhanced} blessing available from me, I know of one other, granted by a solitary {nomad} far west of Svargrond. There are also five different other blessings available, each in a sacred place. ... These blessings are: the {spiritual} shielding, the spark of the {phoenix}, the {embrace} of Tibia, the fire of the {suns} and the wisdom of {solitude}.
You can ask for the blessing of spiritual shielding in the whiteflower temple south of Thais.
You can ask for the blessing of the two suns in the suntower near Ab'Dendriel.
The spark of the phoenix is given by the dwarven priests of earth and fire in Kazordoon.
Talk to the hermit Eremo on the isle of Cormaya about this blessing.
The druids north of Carlin will provide you with the embrace of Tibia.
I know everyone and everything. It is certain that there is another enhanced blessing, the 'heart of the mountain'. Talk to a nomad far to the west of Svargrond, hiding slightly above ground.

greeting: Hello! Tell me what's on your mind. Time is money.

responses: I am the captain of this ship.
I am the captain of this ship.
I'm Karith. I don't belong to a caste any longer, and only serve the Yalahari.
The city was a marvel to behold. It is certain that it have been the many foreigners that ruined it.

greeting: Pshhhht! Not that loud ... but welcome.
