greeting: Welcome on board, |PLAYERNAME|. Where can I {sail} you today?

responses: My boat is ready to bring you to {Venore}, {Port Hope}, {Liberty Bay}, {Yalahar}, {Gray Island}, {Ankrahmun}, {Krailos} or {Issavi}. Or to {Travora} - a place between the worlds.
I am the captain of this ship.
Where do you want to go - {Krailos}, {Venore}, {Port Hope}, {Liberty Bay}, {Ankrahmun}, {Yalahar} or {Gray Island}?
That's where we are.
It's Petros.

greeting: Hello, mighty adventurer |PLAYERNAME|. Can I teach you something you don't know?
farewell: Go and be careful. Remember what you have learned!

responses: I am fine, thank you very much.
My business is knowlegde and it is for free.
I am honored to be teacher in this school.
I run this school, there are other travelling teachers who we call Loremasters.
If you are lucky you'll meet one in your journeys.
My name is Phillip.
It is |TIME|.
I will provide you with all knowledge I have.
Monsters come in different shape and power. It's said there is a zoo in the dwarfs' town.
Dungeons are places of danger and puzzles. In some of them a bright mind will serve you more then a blade.
An interesting place you should consider to visit.
You don't have to thank me, it's only my duty.
To learn about gods, visit the temples and talk to the priests.
The southern king is called Tibianus. He and our queen Eloise are in a constant struggle.
The southern king is called Tibianus. He and our queen Eloise are in a constant struggle.
I don't like rumours.
I don't like rumours.
I don't like rumours.
To learn about weapons read appropriate books or talk to the smiths.
To learn about magic talk to the guild leaders.
Rebellion? What for? We are contend with our situation.
Tod will come and save us all. He will bring freedom and beer to the men of Carlin.
This servant of evil is protected by the dark gods and can't be harmed.
He is a follower of evil. His powers were boosted by a sinister force and he is beyond human restrictions now.
This weapon is said to be very powerful and unique. It was hidden in ancient times and now is thought to be lost.

greeting: Greetings, traveller |PLAYERNAME|. Where do you want me to {fly} you?

responses: I can fly you to {Darashia}, {Issavi}, {Svargrond}, {Kazordoon}, {Zao}, {Femor Hills} or to {Marapur} if you like. Where do you want to go?

greeting: Welcome, young adventurer! Harr! {Deposit} your gold or {withdraw} your money from your bank account. I can also explain the functions of your {bank} account to ya.

responses: Yeah, well, some romantic at work there. Island was reached at dawn, new heroes and adventurers forthcoming, stuff like that.
Ah, wonderful stuff! That and a bottle o' rum, o'course! Harrharr. You have some gold you want to deposit or withdraw, just tell me.
You can deposit and withdraw money from your bank account here.
Once you are on the Tibian mainland, you can access new functions of your bank account, such as changing money, transferring money to other players safely or taking part in house auctions.
Abram Plunderpurse, at your service. <bows>
Here on Dawnport, I run the bank. I keep any gold you deposit safe, so you can't lose it when you're out fighting or dying, heh. Ask me for your balance to learn how much money you've already saved
Arrr. Not a very profitable place.
Arr! I'm a pira... er, I mean <cough> <cough> ... clerk. Banking clerk. That's what I am. You need somethin'? Bank business, p'raps?

Aye, Tibia is a vast world, my friend, with plenty of adventures, harbours, and loot! The Mainland is open to everyone; but there are many beautiful islands and more cities to explore, if you have premium rights and can use a ship.
Once you have reached level 8 here on this isle, you can choose your definite vocation and leave for the Mainland.
There's a choice of four: knight, sorcerer, paladin or druid.
I'm afraid this service is not available to you until you reach the World mainland.
He's an ol' trapper and knows his away around in Tibia, aye. Ask him how a thing works and he'll be sure to have an answer. ...
Gloomy sort. Keeps glaring at me for some reason. Or maybe for no reason, harr. Formidable blacksmith, anyway. Sharpest sword blade I've seen in a long time.

Fetching white beard, I hope I grow one in due time, would impress the younger folk no end! Knowing some sorcerer and druid spells like he does wouldn't come amiss, either. Go to him if you need mage spells.
Could've used his talent to brew up some more explosive runes back in the sea fight against... ah well, you wouldn't know the name anyway. Gotta admit, his potions are good stuff.

<shifts uneasily> Well, maybe I did come across his ship some time. In bad weather. And couldn't do a thing for those poor souls. And anyway, he swam ashore here. So it all worked out in the end, see.
That's Mr Morris to you, friend. Go get yourself a useful thing to do and ask him about a quest, will you.
Harrr, what a dame! Would like to buy her a pint one day. <leers> Unless she kills me with one of her icy looks first. Anyway, decent healer. Can help ya with choosing a vocation.
Aye, what about my name? You don't like it? Well, you don't have to wear it! And I am quite happy with that!
Not my line of trade, friend! Mr Morris next door will tell you what needs doin' around here.
I could swear he looks like that old pal I met back on... ah well, much salt water passed my ship since then. If ye need a spell or two for a knight or paladin, he's the spell teacher to go to.
Arrr. We go wayyyy back, Keeran an' me. Best you ask him, I'm no good at details.
I'm a clerk now. I have better stuff to look after than just a simple key, friend.

farewell: It was a pleasure to help you, |PLAYERNAME|.

responses: Keep in mind you won't find better offers here. Just browse through my wares.

greeting: Welcome, young |PLAYERNAME|! If you are heavily wounded or poisoned, I can {heal} you for free.
walkAway: Remember: If you are heavily wounded or poisoned, I can heal you for free.

responses: This is a special blessing I can bestow upon you once you have obtained at least one of the other blessings and which functions a bit differently. ...
It only works when you're killed by other adventurers, which means that at least half of the damage leading to your death was caused by others, not by monsters or the environment. ...

The {twist of fate} will not reduce the death penalty like the other blessings, but instead prevent you from losing your other blessings as well as the amulet of loss, should you wear one. It costs the same as the other blessings. ...
Would you like to receive that protection for a sacrifice of |PVPBLESSCOST| gold, child?
Fine. You are free to decline my offer.
Ah, you want to replace your adventurer's stone for 30 gold?
Sorry, you don't have enough money.
No problem.
You aren't looking that bad. Sorry, I can't help you. But if you are looking for additional protection you should go on the {pilgrimage} of ashes or get the protection of the {twist of fate} here.

Whenever you receive a lethal wound, your vital force is damaged and there is a chance that you lose some of your equipment. With every single of the five {blessings} you have, this damage and chance of loss will be reduced.

There are five blessings available in five sacred places: the {spiritual} shielding, the spark of the {phoenix}, the {embrace} of Tibia, the fire of the {suns} and the wisdom of {solitude}. Additionally, you can receive the {twist of fate} here.
You can ask for the blessing of spiritual shielding in the whiteflower temple south of Thais.
The druids north of Carlin will provide you with the embrace of Tibia.
You can ask for the blessing of the two suns in the suntower near Ab'Dendriel.
The spark of the phoenix is given by the dwarven priests of earth and fire in Kazordoon.
Talk to the hermit Eremo on the isle of Cormaya about this blessing.

greeting: Yeah, another fool {disturbing} me, what a joy.

I can teach you {healing}, {support} and {attack} spells. I can also tell you which spells are available at your {level}.

I am Magister Puffels, any problem with that?
I have to teach the spells of least importance to some fools. Well, let's face it - to knights.
Guess what, smartypants. I'm the wrong teacher for you. Go see Ursula or Zoltan if you want to broaden your horizon.
The day will come even he makes a fatal casting mistake... I know what I am talking about.
I have no use for such stuff.
Sciences are thriving on this isle.
Fine army that is. Half of them have already deserted.
I heard in Thais the new brand of cheese was... uhm...
I was there once, almost died. The fools there mistook me for an ordinary rat, can you believe that!?
I give nothing for kings, queens... or other people at all.
I don't care about some remote cities.
Bah, the whole Tibia can &#&$*# my #$&*!
Hugo? I heard it was an accident that created this beast.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Welcome, |PLAYERNAME|! Do you feel adventurous? Do you want a weekly {ticket} for the ore wagon system here? You can use it right here to get to the centre of Kazordoon!
Be greeted |PLAYERNAME|! I can smell the scent of a phoenix on you!
farewell: May the fire in your heart never die, |PLAYERNAME|!
walkAway: May the fire in your heart never die.

responses: The Spark of the Phoenix is given by me and by the great geomancer of the local earth temple. Do you wish to receive my part of the Spark of the Phoenix for |BLESSCOST| gold?
Maybe another time.
The ways of the gods are imprehensible to mortals. On the other hand, the elements are raw forces and can be understood and tamed.
I am the head pyromancer of Kazordoon.
My name is Pydar Firefist, Son of Fire, from the Savage Axes.
Ask around. There's a lot to do, jawoll.
That is our world.
It's the fourth age of the yellow flame.
May the great flame devour them all!
Life feeds on fire and ultimately fire will feed on life.
A weapon too powerful to be wielded by mortals. It has to be returned to the fire which gave birth to it.
If he ever dares enter Kazordoon I will gladly dump him into the lava. Tthe sacred flame shall bring justice upon him.
Our city was founded in ancient times. Abandoned by the gods we once fought for, we created a secure haven for our people.
This mountain is said to be the oldest in the world. It is the place where fire and earth meet and separate at the same time.
Bezil and Nezil are buying and selling equipment of all kinds.
Bezil and Nezil are buying and selling equipment of all kinds.
She is the first knight of Kazordoon. She is responsible for teaching our young warriors how to handle an axe.
Etzel is a true master of the elements. He is a role-model for our youngsters, jawoll.
He and his wife are running the Jolly Axeman tavern.
He is a smith. If you are looking for exquisite weapons and armour just talk to him.
She and her husband are running the Jolly Axeman tavern.
Uzgod is a weaponsmith just like those in the old legends.
Though we are through with the so-called gods, Durin, the first dwarf to aquire divine powers of his own, is considered a protector of our race.
Unlike the gods, the elements don't use mortals as toys, A skilled mind can understand and even control them to some extent.
The ways of the gods are imprehensible to mortals. On the other hand, the elements are raw forces and can be understood and tamed.
You can receive the Spiritual Shielding in the Whiteflower Temple south of Thais.
Ask for the Fire of the Suns in the Suntower near Ab'Dendriel.
The druids north of Carlin can provide you with the Embrace of Tibia.
Talk to the hermit Eremo on the isle of Cormaya about this blessing.
You aren't looking that bad. Sorry, I can't help you. But if you are looking for additional protection you should go on the {pilgrimage} of ashes.
I'm the head pyromancer of Kazordoon. I guess you are here for healing or looking for a blessing.

There are five different blessings available in five sacred places. These blessings are: the {spiritual} shielding, the spark of the {phoenix}, the {embrace} of Tibia, the fire of the {suns} and the wisdom of {solitude}.
We are the keepers and shepherds of the elemental force of {fire}.

farewell: COME BACK ANYTIME AND BRING ME TREASURES.
walkAway: COME BACK ANYTIME AND BRING ME TREASURES.

responses: LIKE MY TREASURE? WANNA PICK SOMETHING OUT OF IT?
ALRIGHT. BUT FIRST OF ALL I WANT YOU TO BRING ME SOMETHING IN EXCHANGE. SURPRISE ME....AND IF I LIKE IT, YOU MAY GET WHAT YOU DESERVE.
HAVE YOU SEEN THESE LEGENDARY ITEMS BACK THERE? WHO COULD REFUSE THE CHANCE OF OBTAINING ONE?!? SO WHAT IS YOUR ANSWER?
YOU LITTLE MAGGOT. COME BACK TO ME WHEN YOU CAN HANDLE A FIGHT AGAINST MY KIND.
I HAVE A MISSION FOR YOU BUT YOU NEED TO DIE FIRST AND RETURN AS AN {UNDEAD} CREATURE. COME BACK TO ME WHEN YOU ACHIEVED THIS GOAL.
EVERYTHING YOU CARRY WITH YOU CAN ALSO BE FOUND IN MY {TREASURE}. BRING ME SOMETHING I DON'T OWN!!!
I HAVE A MISSION FOR YOU BUT YOU NEED TO DIE FIRST AND RETURN AS AN {UNDEAD} CREATURE. COME BACK TO ME WHEN YOU ACHIEVED THIS GOAL.
BOON AND BANE. I HAVE CHOSEN THIS LIFE VOLUNTARILLY AND I NEVER REGRET IT. MY {TREASURE} IS GROWING BIGGER EACH DAY.
I can promote you for 20000 gold coins. Do you want me to promote you?
Alright then, come back when you are ready.
The uniforms of our guards and soldiers are of unparraleled quality of course.
I am {Queen} Eloise. It is my duty to reign over this marvellous {city} and the {lands} of the north.
We women try to bring justice and wisdom to all, even to males.
I am Queen Eloise. For you it's 'My Queen' or 'Your Majesty', of course.
I don't care about gossip like a simpleminded male would do.
Soon the whole land will be ruled by women at last!
Thank you, I'm fine.
It's my humble domain.
Sell? Your question shows that you are a typical member of your gender!
We honor the gods of good in our fair city, especially Crunor, of course.
All citizens of Carlin are my subjects. I see them more as my childs, though, epecially the male population.
This beast scared my cat away on my last diplomatic mission in this filthy town.
He is the scourge of the whole continent!
The royal treasure is hidden beyond the grasps of any thieves by magical means.
Go and hunt them! For queen and country!
Visit the church or the townguards for help.
I will call for heroes as soon as the need arises again.
Our city is rich and prospering.
I don't want to talk about 'sewers'.
Dungeons are places where males crawl around and look for trouble.
Feel free to visit our town's magnificent shops.
Don't worry about time in the presence of your Queen.
We need the assistance of heroes now and then. Even males prove useful now and then.
The taxes in Carlin are not high, more a symbol than a sacrifice.
I am the Queen, the only rightful ruler on the continent!
Ask one of the soldiers about that.
Our enemies are numerous. We have to fight vile monsters and have to watch this silly king in the south carefully.
They dare to reject my reign over them!
Isn't our city marvellous? Have you noticed the lovely gardens on the roofs?
My subjects maintain many fine shops. Go and have a look at their wares.
Ask around about them.
The four major guilds are the Knights, the Paladins, the Druids, and the Sorcerers.
They haven't troubled our city lately. I guess, they fear the wrath of our druids.
The paladins are great hunters.
It's a shame that the High Paladin does not reside in Carlin.
The knights of Carlin are the bravest.
The sorcerers have a small isle for their guild. So if they blow something up it does not burn the whole city to ruins.
The druids of Carlin are our protectors and advisors. Their powers provide us with wealth and food.
Carlin is a center of the forces of good, of course.
The forces of evil have a firm grip on this puny city to the south.
The order, Crunor gives the world, is essential for survival.
Chaos is common in the southern regions, where they allow a man to reign over a realm.
A mans tale ... that means 'nonsense', of course.
If you want a reward, go and bring me something this silly King Tibianus wants dearly!
A dusgusting organisation, which could be only created by men.
It is said that he lives on a small island near Edron. Maybe the people there know more about him.

responses: How may I help with your bank account?
Of course, just browse through my wares.
Are you here to get a task or to report you finished task?
Me humble name is Rafzan. Good old goblin name meaning honest, generous and nice person, I swear!
Most goblins so afraid of everything, that they fight everything. Me different. Me just want trade.
You humans are so big, strong, clever and beautiful. Me really feel little and green beside you. Must be sooo fun to be human. You surely always make profit!
To be honest to me human friend, me only heard about it, never seen one. I imagine it's something cute and cuddly.
Swamp is horrible. Slowly eating away at health of poor little goblin. No profit here at all. Me will die poor and desperate, probably eaten by giant mosquitoes.
Beardmen are nasty. Always want to kill little goblin. No trade at all. Not good, not good.

So much to do, so little help. Me poor goblin desperately needs help. Me have a few tasks me need to be done. I can offer you all money I made if you only help me a little with stuff which is easy to strong smart human but impossible for poor, little me.

Me heard Thais is big city with king! Must be strong and clever, to become chief of all humans. Me cannot imagine how many people you have to beat up to become king of all humans. Surely he makes lot of profit in his pretty city.

They are mean and cruel. Humble goblin rarely trades with them. They would rather kill poor me if not too greedy for stuff only me can get them. Still, they rob me of it for a few spare coins and there is noooo profit for poor goblin.

Me job merchant is. Me trade with all kinds of things. Me not good trader though, so you get everything incredibly cheap! You might think me mad, but please don't rip off poor goblin too much. Me has four or five wives and dozens of kids to feed!

Humans so clever. Much, much smarter than poor, stupid goblin. They have big rich town. Goblin lives here poor and hungry. Me so impressed by you strong and smart humans. So much to learn from you. Poor goblin only sees pretty city from afar. Poor goblin too afraid to go there.

Me have seen a gold coin once or twice. So bright and shiny it hurt me poor eyes. You surely are incredibly rich human who has even three or four coins at once! Perhaps you want to exchange them for some things me offer? Just don't rob me too much, me little stupid goblin, have no idea what stuff is worth... you look honest, you surely pay fair price like I ask and tell if it's too cheap.

Furry guys are strange fellows. Always collecting things and stuff. Not easy to make them share, oh there is noooo profit for little, poor me to be made. They build underground dens that can stretch quite far. Rumour has it the corym have strange tunnels that connect their different networks all over the world.

greeting: Hello sweetie. If you need general equipment, stuff like that, let me know.

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares. Remember that if you buy vials of oil, there's a deposit of 5 gold on the empty one.
Just between you and me hun, that woman is a witch. Love potions, poison, voodoo dolls to torture your neighbour. You name it, she got it.
Uh! <whispers> There are ... rumours, you know? About bloody rituals and gruesome curses ... better don't talk aloud about such issues.
Kshh!! No talks about ... the magic folk here ... This might draw their attention.
Hard to imagine that this fat man has such a pretty daughter.
Don't mention HIM in my house you fool. You call evil and mayhem upon us.
I am not dealing with food.
I won't say anything bad about such a high-born person. That's why I remain silent hun .
I run this little pawnshop, hun, but I think you knew that already.
What interest could a king have in this little settlement? If a king cares about us, there must be something important behind all this.
Liberty comes, liberty goes, only the bay stays. Hehe.
I sell torches, candelabra, and oil.
That guy is a shrewd trader, sweetie. I bet even I could learn a thing or two from him.
I have shovels, picks, scythes, bags, ropes, backpacks, plates, scrolls, watches, some lightsources, and other stuff. Just ask me for a {trade}.
Mostly, pirates are bad for business. But sometimes, they are good for business.
Sadly the workers have hardly enough money to buy something to eat and to drink. That is bad also for my business.
The quara are the curse of the sea. Everybody that dares to enrage the sea spirits has to fear their vengeance. They come at night to kidnap people who forgot their lucky charms at home ...
Sometimes those evil beings take the most naughty children to raise them as their own underwater.
Ah, there is little in the world that comes close to the taste of rum.
I heard there is a fine bounty on his head. Would be a shame though to lose such a handsome man to the executioner <winks>.
Sugar lets the money flow. We depend heavily on it.
Thais might be a splendid town, but this is of no use to us here. The situation in our town gets worse with every ship full of foreigners that arrives here.
I heard Venore will soon acquire several trade monopolies that will grant them wealth for centuries.

Well, <lowers the voice to a whisper> there are several people around who know this or that about voodoo and not all of them are up to something good. Just buy some lucky charms from that old hag Charlotta to be on the safe side.
A handsome man but a bit too stiff. I think no one has ever had a chance to see him smiling.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Hello there, mate. Here for a {trade}? My stock's just been refilled.

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.
Richard. Just Richard. Lost my surname with my past in that storm. <winks>
This lovely island here. Once I didn't have to live off squirrels, it became quite enjoyable. Nasty things though live underground, so take care where you tread and ALWAYS have a rope with you!
<shrugs> Never been, mate. Heard it's kinda cute, though.

You know, I really don't want to poke into someone else's private life. Suffice it to say that everyone has chapters of his life he doesn't want to mention. Judging by Coltrayne's looks, we're looking at a trilogy here.
Old Inigo was the one who found me, actually, and brought me to the outpost. I was half starved by then. He taught me how to make better traps and how to fish... I owe much to Inigo.
Can you believe how old he is? He won't say it, but I wouldn't be surprised if he had been around for loooooong time.
Don't talk to ME about squirrels! <shudders> Had to live off them the first days, when they were the only thing to go into my self-made acorn traps. Nasty.
Don't know what to make of him. Great researcher in all Dawnport matters, though. Always has a quest or two where he needs help, if you're looking for adventuring work.
Quiet little lady, her. Knows her way around the isle, looking for herbs and stuff but mostly spends her time in the temple, helping younglings like you choose a vocation.

You know, on the day that ol' pirate decides to make off with all that gold in the bank, I'm gonna come with him. Should be much more fun landing on a strange island with some gold to spend on booze and babes! <winks>
Tries to act tough, but he's quite a witty and decent bloke who wouldn't hurt a fly. We enjoy a good laugh together in the evenings.
I wouldn't be too surprised if the key had been stolen. As often as everybody left it lying around unattended, something like that was bound to happen sooner or later.

I was a carpenter, back on Main. Wanted my own little shop. Didn't sit with the old man. So I shipped to somewhere else. Terrible storm. Woke up on this island. Had to eat squirrels before the adventurers found me and took me in. End of story.

Only the best quality, I assure you. A rope in need is a friend indeed! Imagine you stumble into a rat hole without a rope - heh, your bones will be gnawed clean before someone finds ya! Now, about that rope - ask me for {equipment} to see my wares. <winks>
Of course, just browse through my wares. Or do you want to look only at {containers}, {food}, {equipment}, or {others}?

greeting: |PLAYERNAME| HEHEHEHE! Another fool visits the {tower}! Excellent!
farewell: HEHEHE! I knew you don't have the stomach.

responses: This tower, of course, silly one. It holds my {master'}s {treasure}.
His name is none of your business
I am guarding the treasures of the tower. Only those who pass the {test} of the three sigils may pass.
I am known as the riddler. That is all you need to know.
I am the guardian of the paradox tower.
The key of this tower! You will never find it! A malicious plant spirit is guarding it!.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Hrmpf, I'd say welcome if I felt like lying.
Everyone on this island has gone crazy! Except for me and you, it seems. Let's {trade} like normal people would.
farewell: Promise to come back sometime, will ya?
walkAway: Hey! Don't leave me alone with all these lunatics!

responses: Have you noticed that I'm actually the only rock on this island with a proper job? Those lazy pebbleheads! I'm proud to announce: I'm a trader
No, you got it all wrong! I said I'm stuck between a rock and a hard place!
I can help you buy trading stuff with you. Good for me, good for you. It's a win-win!
Have you noticed that I'm actually the only rock on this island with a proper job? Those lazy pebbleheads! I'm proud to announce: I'm a trader

greeting: Welcome, this is the {Gray Beach temple}, |PLAYERNAME|. Whether you are wounded, poisoned - or wait, don't tell me - yes, this is probably a case of being more emotionally hurt, I will do my best to {heal} you.

responses: This is a special blessing I can bestow upon you once you have obtained at least one of the other blessings and which functions a bit differently. ...
It only works when you're killed by other adventurers, which means that at least half of the damage leading to your death was caused by others, not by monsters or the environment. ...

The {twist of fate} will not reduce the death penalty like the other blessings, but instead prevent you from losing your other blessings as well as the amulet of loss, should you wear one. It costs the same as the other blessings. ...
Would you like to receive that protection for a sacrifice of |PVPBLESSCOST| gold, child?
Fine. You are free to decline my offer.
Ah, you want to replace your adventurer's stone for 30 gold?
Sorry, you don't have enough money.
No problem.
You aren't looking that bad. Sorry, I can't help you. But if you are looking for additional protection you should go on the {pilgrimage} of ashes or get the protection of the {twist of fate} here.

Whenever you receive a lethal wound, your vital force is damaged and there is a chance that you lose some of your equipment. With every single of the five {blessings} you have, this damage and chance of loss will be reduced.

There are five blessings available in five sacred places: the {spiritual} shielding, the spark of the {phoenix}, the {embrace} of Tibia, the fire of the {suns} and the wisdom of {solitude}. Additionally, you can receive the {twist of fate} here.
You can ask for the blessing of spiritual shielding in the whiteflower temple south of Thais.
The druids north of Carlin will provide you with the embrace of Tibia.
You can ask for the blessing of the two suns in the suntower near Ab'Dendriel.
The spark of the phoenix is given by the dwarven priests of earth and fire in Kazordoon.
Talk to the hermit Eremo on the isle of Cormaya about this blessing.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

responses: How may I help with your bank account?

I am Romella and I will be serving you today.
I have many weapons to offer. If you'd like to see my offers, ask me for a {trade}.
I sell and buy weapons. If you'd like to see my offers, ask me for a {trade}.
Our warehouse is the main supplier of the local garrison.
It says the amazons are looking for a certain magical weapon in this area.
I wonder how they finance themselves. I bet they are secretly trading in some strange stuff.
Make sure to buy some extra weapons before facing that one.
I heard the amazons are after it.
Don't go exploring without weapons. Especially you'll need a machete.

My attack spells are: {Apprentice's Strike}, {Buzz}, {Creature Illusion}, {Curse}, {Death Strike}, {Electrify}, {Energy Beam}, {Energy Strike}, {Energy Wave}, {Fire Wave}, {Flame Strike}, {Great Energy Beam}, {Great Fire Wave}, {Ice Strike}, {Ignite}, {Invisible}, {Lightning}, {Scorch}, {Strong Energy Strike}, {Strong Flame Strike} and {Terra Strike}.
My healing spells are: {Cure Poison}, {Intense Healing}, {Light Healing}, {Magic Patch}, {Restoration} and {Ultimate Healing}.

My support spells are: {Animate Dead}, {Cancel Magic Shield}, {Find Fiend}, {Find Person}, {Great Light}, {Haste}, {Levitate}, {Light}, {Magic Rope}, {Magic Shield}, {Strong Haste}, {Summon Creature}, {Summon Sorcerer Familiar} and {Ultimate Light}.

My rune spells are: {Destroy Field} Rune, {Disintegrate} Rune, {Energy Bomb} Rune, {Energy Field} Rune, {Energy Wall} Rune, {Explosion} Rune, {Fire Bomb} Rune, {Fire Field} Rune, {Fire Wall} Rune, {Fireball} Rune, {Great Fireball} Rune, {Heavy Magic Missile} Rune, {Light Magic Missile} Rune, {Poison Field} Rune, {Poison Wall} Rune, {Soulfire} Rune, {Stalagmite} Rune, {Sudden Death} Rune and {Thunderstorm} Rune.

Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Hello, my lad. Sit down, have a drink, why don't you?

Hunter's greeting! I assume you want something from me since you came all the way out here on your own. This is a dangerous place to be, I doubt that all my men will accept strangers like I do. You don't seem to have any problems with that, though.
Welcome to the finest weaponshop in the land, |PLAYERNAME|! Tell me if you're looking for a good {trade}.

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Oh, a customer. Hello |PLAYERNAME|. If you'd like to see my wonderful self-tailored clothes, ask me for a {trade}.

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Welcome to my shop, adventurer |PLAYERNAME|! I {trade} with weapons and armor.

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.
I am the blacksmith. If you need weapons or armor - just ask me.

farewell: Praise the gods, I bid you farewell.

greeting: Hello |PLAYERNAME|, welcome to the fluid and potion {shop} of Edron.
farewell: Good bye, |PLAYERNAME|, please come back soon.
walkAway: Good bye, |PLAYERNAME|, please come back soon.

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares. By the way, if you'd like to join our bonus system for depositing flasks and vial, you have to tell me about that {deposit}.
I sell potions and fluids. If you'd like to see my offers, ask me for a {trade}.

greeting: ...er... hello? Yes...? Well, if... if you have any questions - I am not even here.

responses: I AM THE... I mean... I am - what is a mage, if he is not {mad}? If he isn't... raging? I am... I am just sane. A sane mage.
I am not mad... I- YES, that's the whole problem, isn't it? What's going on, what's happening to me? I don't even know anymore.
Yes, well... I'm taking a break. It will take some time. I don't know how long I just... I want to get away from all this for some time, that's it.
Slime is my mission. Is there anything more important? There isn't. To me. Right now at least.
Slime is my mission. Is there anything more important? There isn't. To me. Right now at least.
You want to help me? HELP me? You? Who... who are you anyway? Ah nevermind.

walkAway: Good bye traveller, and enjoy your stay on Rookgaard.

greeting: Oh, please come in, |PLAYERNAME|. What can I do for you? If you need adventure equipment, ask me for a {trade}.

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares. {Footballs} have to be purchased separately.
Of course, just browse through my wares.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: |PLAYERNAME|. I have been watching your fate for quite some time. It's about time you came here. Do you seek to enter the riven island of Quirefang or travel back from where you came?

responses: My name is Scrutinon. However, there are not many people calling my name nowadays. Not many captains even dare to land on this island. It is too close to {Quirefang}. ...
Most of them do not know this island by that name. Some call it Demon Horn, others the Dragon's Tooth or the Gray Beach as none of them ever came closer than a fair distance. ...

There are drifts and storms surrounding that place that are far too dangerous to navigate through even for the most versed captains. They often sail not closer than to this island here and drop off whoever dares to explore near this dreaded coast.

greeting: Greetings, daring adventurer. If you need a {passage}, let me know.

responses: Where do you want to go? To {Liberty bay} or to {Nargor}?
I am the captain of this ship.
I am the captain of this ship.

greeting: Greetings, daring adventurer. If you need a {passage}, let me know.

responses: Where do you want to go? To {Liberty bay} or to {Meriana}?
I am the captain of this ship.
I am the captain of this ship.
Would you like to learn {wound cleansing} magic spell for free?
Would you like to learn {brutal strike} magic spell for 1000 gold?

Would you like to learn {haste} magic spell for 600 gold?

In this category I have '{Light healing}', '{Wound cleansing}', and '{Cure poison}'.
In this category I have '{Find person}', '{Magic Rope}', '{Levitate}', '{Light}', '{Great Light}', and '{Haste}'.
In this category I have '{Brutal strike}' for knights.
In this category I have '{Conjure Arrow}' for paladins.
I can teach you {healing spells}, {support spells}, {conjure spells} and {attack spells}. What kind of spell do you wish to learn?
I am Ser Tybald. <bows courteously>.
I'm just an adventurer looking for action. Learn from the best! Just ask me about knight and paladin {spells}.

From afar, the island glowed at midnight as if dawn was at hand. A fierce magical light, beckoning, calling out, promising power past redemption - but I am sure {Garamond} has a better idea as to the exact nature of that magical power.
He's a mentor to all young adventurers. If you have a question as to what you should or could do, and how this world works, talk to Inigo, he will be happy to help.
Now there is someone who has had his share of grief. No wonder he came to {Dawnport} to assist raising new heroes for the {Tibian} lands.
Is that old man still teaching magic spells?
We know each other from old, yes. Will we talk about it? No.
That old pirate has settled down now it seems.
He's quite a good cook, which helps improve the mood on bad days. He's a carpenter by trade. Did a good job reinforcing this outpost, too.
It used to be a kind of title in other times, but it serves nicely as a first name here.
Each vocation has their own unique spells to enhance their fighting or defense, which none of the other vocations can use, and which you can only learn at a spell trainer of your vocation.
Most of the major cities are on the Tibian mainland, such as the rich merchant city of Venore, for example. You will find spell teachers for your vocation in almost every major city.

Tibia is the world we live in. Rookgaard is not far off from the Tibian {mainland}, though it's a tricky passage with contrary winds. On the mainland, you will find more adventure, mystery and monsters and can prove yourself a hero or villain.

What a beautiful lady. Do you know the Lay of the White Lady Illadria? ... 'Calm and slender like a birch, soft and white like snow, yet strong and warm like the sun she is.' Well, it fits perfectly. I am her sworn protector.
Ah, still so young and naive about the gods and demons... Still, I envy him his unspoilt beliefs. If I could turn back time... but never mind my ramblings.
Mr Morris has much on his mind and may have been somewhat distracted with regard to that key. He probably just misplaced it, somewhere on top of all those archeological findings he stores.

greeting: Welcome to my fruit and vegetable store, |PLAYERNAME|! Ask me for a {trade} if you'd like to see my wares.

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: The. Slime. Has. Entered. Our. Master. Has. Left! We. Must. Help.

responses: Our. Master. Is. Gone. You. Can. Not. Visit. Him! We. Stand. {Sentry}!
{Master}. Conducted. Experiments. Great. Problems. You. Must. Go!
Of course, just browse through my wares.

farewell: Good bye, |PLAYERNAME|! And remember: No running up and down in the academy!
walkAway: Good bye! And remember: No running up and down in the academy!

responses: Have you brought a dead rat to me to pick up your reward?
HEY! You don't have one! Stop playing tricks on me or I'll give you some extra work!
Go and find some rats to kill!
Do you have a suitable present box for me?
HEY! You don't have one! Stop playing tricks on me or I'll give you some extra work!
You are pretty inexperienced. I think killing rats is a suitable challenge for you. For each fresh {dead rat} I will give you two shiny coins of gold.
This is an island with {vocation} teachers. You can learn all about the different vocations there once you are level 8.
It's |TIME|, so you are late. Hurry!
My name is Seymour, but for you I am 'Sir' Seymour.
At least you know how to address a man of my importance.
I'm the master of this fine {academy}, giving {lessons} to my students.
Oh, which lesson did you want to attend again? Was it {Rookgaard}, {fighting}, {equipment}, {citizens}, the {academy} or the {oracle}?
In a bank you can deposit your earned gold coins safely. Just go downstairs to {Paulie} and ask him to {deposit} your money.
Yes, depositing your money will keep it safe, so it is a good idea to store it in the bank. Of course, you can always withdraw it again.
Well, the {king} doesn't send troops anymore, the {academy} is dreadfully low on money, and the end of the world is pretty nigh. Apart from that I'm reasonably fine, I suppose.
Most of the citizens here are {merchants}. You can give me the name of any non-player character and I will tell you something about him or her.
Merchants buy and sell goods. Just ask them for a {trade} to see what they offer or buy from you.
Trolls are quite nasty monsters which you shouldn't face before level 3 or 4 depending on your {equipment}. Ask the bridge {guards} for their locations!
The guards {Dallheim} and {Zerbrus} protect our village from {monsters} trying to enter. They also mark useful {dungeon} locations on your map.
There are four vocations: {knights}, {paladins}, {sorcerers} and {druids}. You can choose your vocation once you are level 8 and have talked to the {oracle}.
Sorcerers are talented elemental magicians. You will learn all about them once you are level 8 and reached the Island of {Destiny}.
Knights are strong melee fighters. You will learn all about them once you are level 8 and reached the Island of {Destiny}.
Druids are nature magic users and great healers. You will learn all about them once you are level 8 and reached the Island of {Destiny}.
Paladins are swift distance fighters. You will learn all about them once you are level 8 and reached the Island of {Destiny}.
We have a {weapon} and an {armor} shop south of the academy. {Equipment} such as {ropes} are sold to the north-west. {Potions} can be bought to the south. And then there are the {farms}.
The world of Tibia is very large with tons of places to explore. Vast deserts, Caribbean islands, deep jungles, green meadows and jagged mountains await you!
The temple is the place to go when you are very low on {health} or poisoned. Ask {Cipfried} for a heal - he usually notices emergencies by himself.
Your current health is shown by the red bar on the right side. {Death} awaits you if it goes down to zero.
Dying in Tibia is painful, so try to avoid it. You will lose part of your {experience} points and also equipment. Make sure your {health} always stays up!
You gain experience when fighting {monsters}. You can take a look at your skill window to check your progress.
Good monsters to start hunting are {rats}. They live in the {sewers} below the village.
One entrance to the sewers is south of this {academy}. Look for a sewer grate, then use it to climb down.
The academy is the building you are standing in. We have a {library}, a {bank} and the room of the {oracle}.
There are many books in the bookcases around you, unless some naughty kids stole them again. Read them for more and detailed information.
Don't go hunting without proper equipment. You need at least a suitable {weapon}, {armor}, {shield}, {rope} and {shovel}. A {torch} is also good as well as {legs}, a {helmet} and {shoes}.
Make money by killing {monsters} and picking up their {loot}. You can sell many of the things they carry.
Once a monster is dead, you can select 'Open' on the {corpse} to check what's inside. Sometimes they carry {money} or other items which you can sell to {merchants}.
You can even sell some corpses! For example, you can sell fresh dead rats to {Tom} the tanner or me. He also buys other dead creatures, just ask him for a {trade}.
You definitely need a rope to progress in dungeons, else you might end up stuck. Buy one from {Al Dee} or {Lee'Delle}.
A shovel is needed to dig some {dungeon} entrances open. 'Use' it on a loose stone pile to make a hole large enough to enter.
You should not descend into dungeons without proper {equipment}. Once you are all prepared, ask the bridge {guards} for suitable {monsters}.
A torch will provide you with light in dark {dungeons}. 'Use' it to light it. You can buy them from {Al Dee} or {Lee'Delle}.
Well, I could give you valuable {lessons} or some general {hints} about the game, or a small {quest} if you're interested.
The starter armor, a coat, does not protect you well. First of all, earn some money and try to get a sturdy leather armor from {Dixi}'s  or {Lee'Delle}'s shop. Simply ask for a {trade}.
The starter weapon, a club, won't get you far. You should earn some {money} and buy a better weapon such as a sabre from {Obi}'s or {Lee'Delle}'s shop. Simply ask for a {trade}.
A sturdy leather helmet is a good choice for a beginner. You can either buy it from {Dixi} and {Lee'Delle}, or, once you are strong enough, {loot} them from {trolls}.
I fear you have to buy your first shield by yourself. A wooden shield from {Dixi} or {Lee'Delle} is a good choice.
Leather boots are basic shoes which will protect you well. You can either buy them from {Dixi} and {Lee'Delle}, or, once you are strong enough, {loot} them from {trolls}.
Leather legs might be a good basic protection. You can buy them from {Dixi} or {Lee'Delle}. Or, once you are strong enough, hunt {trolls}. They sometimes carry them in their {loot}.
Many monsters, such as rabbits or deer, are excellent food providers. You can also buy food from {Willie} or {Billy}, the farmers.
Paying for your Tibia account will turn it into a premium account. This means access to more areas and functions of the game as well as other neat features.
Hail to King Tibianus! Long live our king! Not that he cares for an old veteran who is stuck on this godforsaken island...
Use a small health potion in case of emergencies to fill up around 75 health points. You can buy them at {Lily}'s shop. She also has {antidote} potions.
Some monsters poison you. To heal poison, use an antidote potion on yourself. Buy them at {Lily}'s store.
Rookgaard is the name of this {village} as well as of the whole {island}. It belongs to the kingdom of {Thais}, in our world which is called {Tibia}.
The island is separated into a {premium} side and a non-premium side. On both sides you will find {dungeons}, but the premium side tends to be a little less crowded with other players.
The city of Thais is reigned by King Tibianus. Of course, there are many other cities but you will learn about them later.
The most important places in this village are the {temple}, the different {shops}, the {academy} and the {bridges}.
There is a bridge to the north and one to the west which lead outside the village. You should only leave once you are well {equipped} and at least level 2.
You can leave for mainland once you are level 8. To do so talk to the {oracle}.
You have to fight {monsters} to train your {skills} and {level}. If you lose {health}, eat {food} to regain it or use a {potion}.
The more you fight with a weapon, the better will be your skill handling this weapon. Don't worry about that right now though, this will become important once you have a {vocation}.
Once you gained enough experience for a level, you will advance. This means - among other things - more {health} points, a faster walking speed and more strength to carry things.
The farms are west of here. You can buy and sell {food} there which you need to regain {health}.
To attack a rat, simply click on it in your battle list. Make sure that you have proper {equipment}, though! Also, I give you 2 gold coins for each {dead rat}.
I personally don't have anything to trade, but you can ask {merchants} for a trade. That will open a window where you can see their offers and the things they buy from you.
Well, I could give you valuable {lessons} or some general {hints} about the game, or a small {quest} if you're interested.
Obi sells and buys {weapons}. You can find his shop south of the academy.
Norma has recently opened a bar here meaning she sells drinks and snacks. Nothing of importance to you, young student.
Oh, let's not talk about Loui.
A fine and helpful man. Without him, many new adventurers would be quite clueless.
This is Tom the tanner's mother. Other than that, I don't think she is of importance.
Al Dee has a general {equipment} store in the north-western part of the village. He sells useful stuff such as {ropes}.
A traveller from the {main} continent. I wonder what brought her here. No one comes here of his own free will.
Billy is {Willie}'s cousin, but he has his farm on the {premium} side of the village.
Willie is a fine farmer, although he is short-tempered. He sells and buys {food}.
A humble monk living in the {temple} south of here. He can heal you if you are wounded or poisoned.
Dixi sells and buys {armors}, {shields}, {helmets} and {legs}. You can find her shop south of the academy, just go up the stairs in {Obi}'s shop.
A mysterious druid who lives somewhere in the wilderness. He sells small health {potions} just like {Lily}.
Lee'Delle's shop is in the western part of town, on the {premium} side. She sells everything cheaper.
In the southern part of town, Lily sells {potions} which might come in handy once you are deep in a dungeon and need {health}.
The oracle is a mysterious being just upstairs. It will bring you to the {Island of Destiny} to choose your {vocation} once you are level 8.
Yes, Paulie is very important. He is the local {bank} clerk.
Sir Seymour, yes, that's me.
Tom the tanner buys fresh {corpses}, minotaur leather and paws. Always good to make some {money} if you can carry the corpses there fast enough.
He's the guard on the north {bridge} and a great fighter. He can show you {monster} locations. Just ask him about monsters!
He's the guard on the west {bridge} and a great fighter. He can show you {monster} locations. Just ask him about monsters!

greeting: Hello |PLAYERNAME|! You're late, do you have no concept of time? My mission is of utmost importance. If you are not interested in helping me, you might as well just leave.

responses: I heard of a device which would allow man to sail the sea of light. I call it the {Lightboat}, probably the most important invention in our history. And I will not rest until I have found a way to put the plan of its inventor into action.

I heard of a device which would allow man to sail the sea of light. I call it the {Lightboat}, probably the most important invention in our history. And I will not rest until I have found a way to put the plan of its inventor into action.

I heard of a device which would allow man to sail the sea of light. I call it the {Lightboat}, probably the most important invention in our history. And I will not rest until I have found a way to put the plan of its inventor into action.
The sea of light is what I call the endless arrangement of shiny stars in the night sky. If we fail to complete the {magic device}, science will probably never uncover its secrets.

I found the cause of the initial failure. A rare component, a mirror crystal was used to store magical energy. Miscalculations within the construction damaged this fragile power source. The unleashed energy must have been devastating.

The people of Edron were digging there for minerals and gold. It seemed all that work was of no avail when they finally hit the motherlode. Gems, rare crystals and... water. So much that it flooded the whole system. And not a single soul escaped.

I heard rumours of a fabled creature whose traces were found on an island near Carlin. It lives solely to collect all kinds of rare gems, crystals and minerals. Offering an item it does not already own, reportedly allows passage into its lair.
Of course, just browse through my wares.
I can't reveal much to you right now as time is of the essence. I can tell you though that the glooth plant and the whole town are in danger and we have to act to stop a {madman} and his schemes.
I defeated him there in the past. It has been abandoned for many {years}, but I suspected him to return from the dead eventually, as he has done so often before.
The creature is powerful but not very clever. It seems it can be easily {enraged} and then it would likely let slip all caution and would relentlessly attack without regard for its safety.
I used the time to prepare. You'll find my talking tubes all the {way} to his lair, and I can provide you with vital information about the things ahead.

greeting: How could you sneak up on me like this? I thought you were one of THEM! Well, since you are not, what brings you to this wilderness?
farewell: Take good care of yourself traveller. Would be a shame to lose such a courageous wanderer to those green monsters.
walkAway: Take good care of yourself traveller. Would be a shame to lose such a courageous wanderer to those green monsters.

greeting: Hi! You look like someone on a {mission}.

responses: Me put name away name long ago. Now only blind prophet of ape people are.
Me is who in dreams speak to holy banana. Me divine the will of banana.
Me {prophet} and {guardian} is.
Me is who in dreams speak to holy banana. Me divine the will of banana.
Me guard the forbidden land behind the great palisade. If any want to enter, he must ask me for transport.
Me guard the forbidden land behind the great palisade. If any want to enter, he must ask me for transport.
Good ape he is. Has to work hard to make other apes listen but you helped a lot.

Our holy ancestor he is. Big as mountain. Lizards say they built palisade to keep him but we not believe ... We think Bong palisade built to have peace from pesky lizards. We respect peace of Bong, keep people away from forbidden land.
The lizards evil and vengeful are. Ape people on guard must be.
Our people a lot to learn have. One day we might live in peace with you hairless apes, who knows.
Me not know. Me seldom have visions of not banana related objects.
Hairless apes strange people are.

farewell: Good bye. May the gods watch over your path.

greeting: Hello, my sparring buddy. We should have another fight sometimes. I think you may have earned a little reward.

responses: Times have changed <sigh>. In the past dragons were feared and respected. Only the {demons} rivalled our notoriety.
Those upstarts! I wonder why would anyone care about them. They lack our style. For them it is all about brute force and showing-off.
Breathing fire is an art! Instead of setting everything on fire, you exhale a cone of fire to give a worthy opponent a chance to avoid it.
Your lives are so short and meaningless and yet you are here! And as a race you even have your own history and remember things with the help of {books}, which amazes me.
I like the idea of books so much that I acquired a human servant to record my memoirs. You find a copy somewhere in my lair.
Lava is only fun as long as it doesn't harden - then it turns into an annoyance.

greeting: Greetings, dear guest. If you are interested in paperware such as books or scrolls, ask me for a trade.

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.

farewell: COME BACK WHEN YOU ARE PREPARED TO FACE YOUR DESTINY!
walkAway: COME BACK WHEN YOU ARE PREPARED TO FACE YOUR DESTINY!

responses: I am Charkahn the Slayer! The immortal father of the {orcs} and master of this {hive}.
The orcs are the bearers of Blogs rage. This makes us the ultimate fighters and the most powerful of all races.
The orcs are the bearers of Blogs rage. This makes us the ultimate fighters and the most powerful of all races.
I can sense the presence and the feelings of my underlings and {minions}. I embrace the rage of the horde.
The orcish horde of this hive is under my control. I sense their emotions and their needs and provide them with the leadership they need to focus their hate and rage
Hate and rage are the true blessings of Blog, since they are powerful weapons. They give the hive strength. I provide them with direction and focus.
The Raging One blessed us with his burning hate. We are truly his children and therefore {divine}.
To conquer, to destroy and to dominate. Orcs are born to rule the {world}.
One day I will rule the world, even when turned into a {slime}.
Pah! Don't mock me, mortal! This shape is a curse which the evil {djinn} bestowed upon me!
This cursed djinn king! I set him free from an enchanted {lamp}, and he {cheated} me!
Because I freed him he granted me three wishes. He was true to his word in the first two {wishes}.
He built this fortress over Uldrek's grave within a single night. Also, he granted me my second wish and gave me immortality. Test it and try to kill me if you want. Har Har!

I wished to father more healthy and fertile children as any orc has ever done. But the djinn cheated me and made me a slime! Then he laughed at me and left for his abandoned fortress in the {Deathwish} Mountains.

His ancient fortress on Darama was deserted as the evil Djinn fled this world after his imprisonment. Now the time has come for the evil Djinns to return to their master although this will certainly awaken the {good Djinn} too.
I will not share anything more about that topic with you {paleskins}.
You are as ugly as maggots, although not quite as as tasty.

greeting: Be greeted, dear visitor. Come and {stay} ... a while.
walkAway: Yes, flee from death. But know it shall be always one step behind you.

responses: It's my curse to be the eternal {guardian} of this ancient {place}.
I'm the {guardian} of the {SEVENTH} and final seal. The seal to open the last door before ... but perhaps it's better to see it with your own eyes.
It served as a temple, a source of power and ... as a sender for an ancient {race} which lived a long time ago and has long been forgotten.
The race that built this edifice came to this place from the stars. They ran from an enemy even more horrible than themselves. But they carried the {seed} of their own destruction in them.
This ancient race was annihilated by its own doings, that's all I know. Aeons have passed since then, but the sheer presence of this {complex} is still defiling and desecrating this area.

Its constructors were too strange for you or even me to understand. We don't know what this ... thing they built was supposed to be good for. I feel a constant twisting and binding of souls, though, that is probably only a side-effect.
The place you know as the Ghostlands had a different name once ... and many names after. Too many to remember them all.
They are my maidens. They give me comfort in my eternal watch over the last seal.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Welcome in the name of the gods, pilgrim |PLAYERNAME|!
farewell: Good bye, |PLAYERNAME|. May the gods be with you to guard and guide you, my child!

responses: A blessed stake? That is a strange request, my child. Maybe Quentin knows more, he is one of the oldest monks after all.
You aren't looking that bad. Sorry, I can't help you. But if you are looking for additional protection you should go on the {pilgrimage} of ashes or get the protection of the {twist of fate} here.
I am a priest of the great pantheon.
The teachings of Crunor tell us to honor life and not to harm it.
It is my mission to bring the teachings of the gods to everyone.
It is my mission to bring the teachings of the gods to everyone.
My name is Tibra. Your soul tells me that you are |PLAYERNAME|.
Queen Eloise is wise to listen to the proposals of the druidic followers of Crunor.
The grace of the gods must be earned, it cannot be bought!
The world of Tibia is the creation of the gods.
Now, it is |TIME|.
There's something strange in its neighbourhood. But whom we gonna call for help if not the gods?
Remind: Not everything you call monster is evil to the core!
The mythical blade was hidden in ancient times. Its said that powerful wards protect it.
The fallen one should be mourned, not feared.
Only a man can fall as low as he did. His soul rotted away already.
The gods of good guard us and guide us, the gods of evil want to destroy us and steal our souls!
The gods we call the good ones are Fardos, Uman, the Elements, Suon, Crunor, Nornur, Bastesh, Kirok, Toth, and Banor.
Fardos is the creator. The great obsever. He is our caretaker.
Uman is the positive aspect of magic. He brings us the secrets of the arcane arts.
Air is one of the primal elemental forces, sometimes worshipped by tribal shamans.
Fire is one of the primal elemental forces, sometimes worshipped by tribal shamans.
Sula is the essence of the elemental power of water.
Suon is the lifebringing sun. He observes the creation with love.
Crunor, the great tree, is the father of all plantlife. He is a prominent god for many druids.
Nornur is the mysterious god of fate. Who knows if he is its creator or just a chronist?
Bastesh, the deep one, is the goddess of the sea and its creatures.
Kirok, the mad one, is the god of scientists and jesters.
Toth, Lord of Death, is the keeper of the souls, the guardian of the afterlife.
Banor, the heavenly warrior, is the patron of all fighters against evil. He is the gift of the gods to inspire humanity.
The gods we call the evil ones are Zathroth, Fafnar, Brog, Urgith, and the Archdemons!
Zathroth is the destructive aspect of magic. He is the deciver and the thief of souls.
Fafnar is the scorching sun. She observes the creation with hate and jealousy.
Brog, the raging one, is the great destroyer. The berserk of darkness.
The bonemaster Urgith is the lord of the undead and keeper of the damned souls.
The demons are followers of Zathroth. The cruelest are known as the ruthless seven.
I don't want to talk about that subject!
It's my duty to protect the city.

greeting: Oh, hello! It's a pleasure to see a visitor from the continent.
Welcome to my little shop, adventurer! First read my blackboards.

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Uhm oh hello |PLAYERNAME|... not so loud please... my {head}... What ... do you {want}?
farewell: Yes, goodbye |PLAYERNAME|, just leave me alone.

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares.
I am... a traveler. Just leave me alone if you have nothing {interesting} to talk about.
I am... a traveler. Just leave me alone if you have nothing {interesting} to talk about.
Uhhh ohhhh one of the beers yesterday must have been bad.
My Name? I am To... ahm... hum... My name is {Hugo}.
Yes, that's my name of course.
Uh .. I... I met a Todd on the road. He told me he was traveling to Venore, look there for your Todd.
I love that city.
I never was there. Now leave me alone.
Resistance is futile... uhm... I wonder where I picked that saying up. Oh my head...
I don't know anything about money, missing or not.
He often comes here. But his constant confusion gives me a worse headache than Frodo's beer. I rather avoid him.
Uhm, never heard about him... and you can't prove otherwise.
Thats a common name, perhaps I met a William, not sure about that.
Of course, just browse through my wares.
Sure, check what I buy.
My name is Tom the tanner.
I'm the local {tanner}. I buy fresh animal {corpses}, tan them, and convert them into fine leather clothes which I then sell to {merchants}.
{Dixi} and {Lee'Delle} sell my leather clothes in their shops.
That's my job. It can be dirty at times but it provides enough income for my living.
Do I look like a tourist information centre? Go ask someone else.
Help? I will give you a few gold coins if you have some fresh dead {animals} for me. Note the word {fresh}.
Fresh means: shortly after their death.
Much to do these days.
Good monsters to start with are rats. They live in the {sewers} under the village of {Rookgaard}.
Dungeons can be dangerous without proper {equipment}.
You need at least a {backpack}, a {rope}, a {shovel}, a {weapon}, an {armor} and a {shield}.
Sorry, I haven't been outside for a while, so I don't know.
Troll leather stinks. Can't use it.
I don't buy orcs. Their skin is too scratchy.
Nope, sorry, don't sell that. Go see {Al Dee} or {Lee'Delle}.
Nope, sorry, don't sell that. Ask {Dixi} or {Lee'Delle}.
Nope, sorry, don't sell that. Ask {Obi} or {Lee'Delle}.
I'm buying fresh {corpses} of rats, rabbits and wolves. I don't buy half-decayed ones. If you have any for sale, {trade} with me.
He's an apple polisher.
Now that's an interesting woman.
He's a better cook than his cousin {Willie}, actually.
I kinda like him. At least he says what he thinks.
Yep.
He sticks his nose too much in books.
My mother?? Did you meet my mother??
I don't have a problem with him.
Typical pencil pusher.
It's in the academy, just above Seymour. Go there once you are level 8 to leave this place.
He is such a hypocrite.
I like her beer.
She buys my fine leather clothes.
I wonder what spectacular monsters he has found.
Her nose is a little high in the air, I think. She never shakes my hand.
I wonder if he's angry because his potion monopoly fell.
I'm not what you'd call a 'believer'.
He's okay.

responses: This is a special blessing I can bestow upon you once you have obtained at least one of the other blessings and which functions a bit differently. ...
It only works when you're killed by other adventurers, which means that at least half of the damage leading to your death was caused by others, not by monsters or the environment. ...

The {twist of fate} will not reduce the death penalty like the other blessings, but instead prevent you from losing your other blessings as well as the amulet of loss, should you wear one. It costs the same as the other blessings. ...
Would you like to receive that protection for a sacrifice of |PVPBLESSCOST| gold, child?
Fine. You are free to decline my offer.
Ah, you want to replace your adventurer's stone for 30 gold?
Sorry, you don't have enough money.
No problem.
You aren't looking that bad. Sorry, I can't help you. But if you are looking for additional protection you should go on the {pilgrimage} of ashes or get the protection of the {twist of fate} here.

Whenever you receive a lethal wound, your vital force is damaged and there is a chance that you lose some of your equipment. With every single of the five {blessings} you have, this damage and chance of loss will be reduced.

There are five blessings available in five sacred places: the {spiritual} shielding, the spark of the {phoenix}, the {embrace} of Tibia, the fire of the {suns} and the wisdom of {solitude}. Additionally, you can receive the {twist of fate} here.
You can ask for the blessing of spiritual shielding in the whiteflower temple south of Thais.
The druids north of Carlin will provide you with the embrace of Tibia.
You can ask for the blessing of the two suns in the suntower near Ab'Dendriel.
The spark of the phoenix is given by the dwarven priests of earth and fire in Kazordoon.
Talk to the hermit Eremo on the isle of Cormaya about this blessing.
Of course, just browse through my wares.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

greeting: Good hunting! I can offer you some lovely {rewards} for finishing prey hunting tasks! Furthermore I can tell you how many hunting task points (HTP) you actually {have} and you have already {spent}.
Yes? What may I do for you, |PLAYERNAME|? Bank business, perhaps?

responses: We can {change} money for you. You can also access your {bank account}.

There are three different coin types in Tibia: 100 gold coins equal 1 platinum coin, 100 platinum coins equal 1 crystal coin. So if you'd like to change 100 gold into 1 platinum, simply say '{change gold}' and then '1 platinum'.
We can {change} money for you. You can also access your {bank account}.
Your bank account will be used automatically when you want to {rent} a house or place an offer on an item on the {market}. Let me know if you want to know about how either one works.
You can check the {balance} of your bank account, {deposit} money or {withdraw} it. You can also {transfer} money to other characters, provided that they have a vocation.

I'm afraid this service is not available to you until you reach the World mainland.

greeting: Greetings and Banor be with you, |PLAYERNAME|! May I interest you in a {trade} for weapons, ammunition or armor?

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares. If you're only interested in {distance} equipment, let me know.

greeting: Hiho |PLAYERNAME|. I hope you're here to {trade}.

responses: Ya take a good look.
Haven't they taught you anything at school? Ask for a {trade} if you want to trade.
Help yourself. Or ask the other {citizens}, I don't have time for that.
Fine enough.
Willie.
I'm a farmer and a cook.
I try out old and new {recipes}. You can sell all {food} to me.
I'd love to try a banana pie but I lack the {bananas}. If you get me one, I'll reward you.
Which one?
This island would be wonderful if there weren't a constant flood of newcomers.
If I were you, I'd stay here.
I know how to spell.
I'm a magician in the kitchen.
I'm not in the weapon business, so stop disturbing me.
I'm glad that we don't see many officials here.
I'm a farmer, not a preacher.
What about them? Do you live there?
I've got no time for your dungeon nonsense.
My cousin {Billy} cooks rat stew. Yuck! Can you imagine that?
Are you afraid of monsters? I bet even the sight of a {rat} would let your knees tremble. Hahaha.
Do I look like a clock?
I'm a farmer, not a preacher.
Can't stand him.
Quite a babe.
Don't ever mention his name again! He can't even {cook}!
Our little monkey.
Uhm, fine guy I think.
Boring little girl.
Overrated.
She thinks she owns this island with her underpriced offers.
I don't like hippie girls.
Leave me alone with that guy.
About time we got a bar here.
This old guy has only money on his mind.
Hopefully it gets you off this island soon so you can stop bugging me.
Uptight and correct in any situation.
If he wants to sacrifice all his free time for beginners, fine with me. Then they don't disturb me.
This joke of a man thinks he is sooo important.
Decent guy.
Yeah, so?
Overrated.
Too old to be interesting for me.
Have you found a banana for me?
Are you trying to mess with me?!
Too bad.
No part of clan. Me prefer company of {precious}. Or mirror image. Always nice to see pretty me!
Me not like minos either. Huge mean bullies! Woblin peaceful here.
Me not like trolls. Big furry stinky trolls!
Mean short people, make Woblin's clan do slave work for them.
Ugly stomping giants, always busy hammering stone.
What you on quest for? Go leave Woblin alone with {precious}.
Fish good. Fish quiet and tasty. Woblin try to catch fishes, but... hm you could earn yourself {reward}!
Me not trade! Goods my precious now! By old goblin law all that one has in his pockets for two days is family heirloom! Me no part with my goods ... hm unless you provide Woblin with some {reward}!
Lost what? Key?

walkAway: Remember: If you are heavily wounded or poisoned, I can heal you for free.

responses: This is a special blessing I can bestow upon you once you have obtained at least one of the other blessings and which functions a bit differently. ...
It only works when you're killed by other adventurers, which means that at least half of the damage leading to your death was caused by others, not by monsters or the environment. ...

The {twist of fate} will not reduce the death penalty like the other blessings, but instead prevent you from losing your other blessings as well as the amulet of loss, should you wear one. It costs the same as the other blessings. ...
Would you like to receive that protection for a sacrifice of |PVPBLESSCOST| gold, child?
Fine. You are free to decline my offer.
Ah, you want to replace your adventurer's stone for 30 gold?
Sorry, you don't have enough money.
No problem.
A blessed stake? That is a strange request. Maybe Quentin knows more, he is one of the oldest monks after all.
You aren't looking that bad. Sorry, I can't help you. But if you are looking for additional protection you should go on the {pilgrimage} of ashes or get the protection of the {twist of fate} here.

Whenever you receive a lethal wound, your vital force is damaged and there is a chance that you lose some of your equipment. With every single of the five {blessings} you have, this damage and chance of loss will be reduced.

There are five blessings available in five sacred places: the {spiritual} shielding, the spark of the {phoenix}, the {embrace} of Tibia, the fire of the {suns} and the wisdom of {solitude}. Additionally, you can receive the {twist of fate} here.
You can ask for the blessing of spiritual shielding in the whiteflower temple south of Thais.
The druids north of Carlin will provide you with the embrace of Tibia.
You can ask for the blessing of the two suns in the suntower near Ab'Dendriel.
The spark of the phoenix is given by the dwarven priests of earth and fire in Kazordoon.
Talk to the hermit Eremo on the isle of Cormaya about this blessing.

<nods> At your service, |PLAYERNAME|, protecting the {village} from {monsters}.

responses: Zerbrus, at your service.
I'm the guard of this bridge. I defend Rookgaard against the beasts of the {wilderness} and the {dungeons}, and even of the stinking {sewers}.
My duty is eternal. Time is of no importance.
Before you fight monsters, deposit your money there. If you die, you won't lose it if it's save in the bank.
Fine.
I have to stay here, sorry. But I can {heal} you if you are wounded. I have also valuable information about {monsters}.
I'm an expert concerning the wildlife and {monsters} on this island.
I'm not a gossiper, but if you want to know something about a certain Rookgaardian, ask.
They're not that dangerous, but don't let yourself get cornered by many of them. They still can hurt.
There are two types of spiders on this island: normal spiders and poison spiders. As their name says, the latter ones can poison you which will cause a constant loss of your health. Beware of them!
Once you have upgraded your starter {equipment} a little - maybe leather stuff - and got a better weapon as well as a shield, you should be able to kill trolls.
You need a stronger weapon than the starter club to kill wolves. Maybe a {sabre} or a short {sword} will do.
The orcs have a hideout under the troll cave, just descend deep. Still, be very careful!
Minotaurs are very strong. You will need a group of people or at least level 6 and good equipment to be able to kill them.
They are a little tougher than they look like, but they're still good beginner monsters.
The skeletons on this island are usually fairly hidden. They are often found near graves and always underground. Beware of their evil life drain.
There is a bear cave close to this bridge. Be careful, though, they hit hard!
Wasp hives exist only behind the north bridge, so ask {Dallheim} for information. I'm glad there are no wasps here. They always make me jump.
Snakes can poison you, too, but their poison is usually not as dangerous as that of poison spiders. Just be careful and kill them fast.
Premium warriors pay. They gain many advantages this way.
HAIL TO THE KING!
It's north of the temple.
A place of protection. Nothing can harm you there.
I can mark you some dungeons on your map if you ask me about {monsters} suited for your level.
The sewers are crowded with {rats}. They make fine targets for young heroes.
There are {wolves}, {bears}, {snakes}, {deer}, and {spiders} in the wilderness past this bridge.
I'm a follower of {Banor}.
The heavenly warrior! Read {books} to learn about him.
You will learn about magic soon enough.
In the world of Tibia, many challenges await the brave adventurer.
There are many books in the {academy}. Theory can be just as important as practice.
It's my duty to protect this village and its {citizens}.
It looks like you have mastered the drill.
Sorry, I don't trade. Ask the shop owners for a trade instead.
I don't approve of her opening a bar. Those drunken adventurers stumbling over the bridge at nighttime are not especially helpful in improving the situation.
Such a chicken-hearted person.
He sells {weapons}. His shop is south west of town, close to the {temple}.
He dedicated his life to welcoming newcomers to this island.
She has her own problems.
There's something about this guy I don't like.
She's very attractive. Too bad my duty leaves me no time to date her.
He guards the temple and can heal you if you are badly injured or poisoned, just like me.
She's {Obi's} granddaughter and deals with {armors} and {shields}. Her shop is south west of town, close to the temple.
One of these reclusive druids.
Her shop is just north of the bridge. She really has good offers.
Gentle girl, but a little to esoteric for my taste. However, her {potions} might come in handy.
You can find the oracle on the top floor of the {academy}, just above {Seymour}. Go there when you are level 8.
The local {bank} clerk.
His job of teaching young heroes is important for the survival of all of us.
He's the local tanner. You could try selling fresh corpses or leather to him.
He does a fine job, protecting the north bridge. He's been on duty longer than me and knows the north side of Rookgaard well.
At your service.
He's a farmer. A little more friendly than his cousin {Willie}, he also buys and sells {food}.
He's quite rude. Also, he buys and sells {food}.
You aren't looking really bad. Eat some food to regain strength.
Of course, just browse through my wares.

farewell: Good bye |PLAYERNAME|, may Uman bless you!.

greeting: Ah, the wind brings in another visitor. Welcome, dear guest. Are you looking for a {passage} with my carpet?
farewell: Good bye. Recommend us if you were satisfied with our service.

greeting: Ah our human {alliez} have finally arrived! I've already feared zat ze emperor'z forzez had caught you. Your rezilienze and prowezz are imprezzive.
Ah, ze human everyone iz talking about. Your victory over ze champion waz quite imprezzive. ...
Alzough, for many ziz only provez what a huge zreat you blank-zkinz ztill poze. What do you {want}?
Yes? What may I do for you, |PLAYERNAME|? Bank business, perhaps?

responses: How may I help with your bank account?

greeting: Welcome |PLAYERNAME|, student of the arcane arts. I teach the fiercest {spells} available.

responses: Would you like to learn {blood rage} magic spell for 8000 gold?
Would you like to learn {eternal winter} magic spell for 8000 gold?
Would you like to learn {hell's core} magic spell for 8000 gold?
Would you like to learn {protector} magic spell for 6000 gold?
Would you like to learn {rage of the skies} magic spell for 6000 gold?
Would you like to learn {sharpshooter} magic spell for 8000 gold?
Would you like to learn {swift foot} magic spell for 6000 gold?
Would you like to learn {ultimate energy strike} magic spell for 15000 gold?
Would you like to learn {ultimate flame strike} magic spell for 15000 gold?
Would you like to learn {ultimate ice strike} magic spell for 15000 gold?
Would you like to learn {ultimate terra strike} magic spell for 15000 gold?
Would you like to learn {wrath of nature} magic spell for 6000 gold?
I can teach you {support} and {attack} spells. What kind of spell do you wish to learn? I can also tell you which spells are available at your {level}.
Support spells: {Blood Rage} (Knight), {Protector} (Knight), {Sharpshooter} (Paladin), {Swift Foot} (Paladin).

Attack spells: {Eternal Winter} (Druid), {Hell's Core} (Sorcerer), {Rage of the Skies} (Sorcerer), {Ultimate Energy Strike} (Sorcerer), {Ultimate Flame Strike} (Sorcerer), {Ultimate Ice Strike} (Druid), {Ultimate Terra Strike} (Druid), {Wrath of Nature} (Druid).
I have spells for level {100}, {90}, {60} and {55}.
For level 100 I have {Ultimate Energy Strike} for 15000 gold and {Ultimate Ice Strike} for 15000 gold.
For level 90 I have {Ultimate Flame Strike} for 15000 gold and {Ultimate Terra Strike} for 15000 gold.
For level 60 I have {Blood Rage} for 8000 gold, {Eternal Winter} for 8000 gold, {Hell's Core} for 8000 gold and {Sharpshooter} for 8000 gold.
For level 55 I have {Protector} for 6000 gold, {Rage of the Skies} for 6000 gold, {Swift Foot} for 6000 gold and {Wrath of Nature} for 6000 gold.

greeting: Oh, please come in, |PLAYERNAME|. What can I do for you? If you need adventure equipment, ask me for a {trade}.

responses: Of course, just browse through my wares. {Footballs} have to be purchased separately.
I sell equipment for your adventure! Just ask me for a {trade} to see my wares.

greeting: Another visitor to this constricted, cosy, calm realm, perfect except for an {exit}. Muhaha.

responses: I wait. I wait for someone like you to come here. I wait for them to grow disconsolate. I wait for them to despair. And I wait for them to die. Muhahaha.
I am Zumtah, Zumtah the impeccable, Zumtah the marvellous, Zumtah the... the... eternal.
The land you are currently dwelling in, human. Don't you have any sense of your surroundings?
I have seen many of them. I have seen many of them die. In here, with me. Perhaps you will be pleased to meet them. Not long and you will join their ranks. Muhaha.
Pesky creatures. Many of them have been brought here, many of them died here. Humans, lizards, beasts, they all die the same. Down here, with me. Muhahaha.
What? What do you mean by that?
Hmm, an old one. I don't care much about politics or power, as here, he has none. Here, only I have power. Muhaha.
What are you talking about, such things do not matter down here. Down here alone, isolated and broken. Muhaha.
